17	// Package anthropic implements pkg/dispatch.Subagent against Anthropic's
18	// Claude Code CLI. Per D-C1 (the provider firewall) all Anthropic-specific
19	// code lives here, NOT in pkg/dispatch, pkg/controller, or pkg/dag â the
20	// firewall is enforced at build time by tools/analyzers/providerfirewall.
21	//
22	// HARN-06 decision (03-RESEARCH Â§"Alternatives Considered"): we shell out to
23	// the `claude` CLI rather than embedding the Anthropic Go SDK. The CLI bundles
24	// the agent loop, hooks, MCP, skills, and bash/file tools that would otherwise
25	// have to be re-implemented in Go. Phase 3 does not depend on the Anthropic
26	// Go SDK module.
27	//
28	// CLAUDE.md anti-pattern guardrails baked in:
29	//   - NEVER mount the host claude config dir â the --bare flag (RESEARCH
30	//     Â§"Critical new finding") skips auto-discovery of hooks/skills/plugins/MCP
31	//     and any CLAUDE-doc auto-memory, so the per-Pod runtime is hermetic.
32	//   - NEVER use OAuth headless â claude-code#29983, #7100 break it. We pin
33	//     ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the signed token minted by the controller and
34	//     validated by the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4).
35	//   - NEVER embed an LLM API key directly â the API key lives only in the
36	//     credproxy sidecar; this binary sees a short-lived HMAC token.
37	package anthropic
38	
39	import (
40		"bytes"
41		"context"
42		"encoding/json"
43		"errors"
44		"fmt"
45		"maps"
46		"os"
47		"os/exec"
48		"path/filepath"
49		"sort"
50		"strings"
51		"time"
52	
53		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/common"
54		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
55	)
56	
57	// vendorSentinel is the Provider.Vendor value this binary accepts. The
58	// envelope.Provider.Vendor field is the compile-time agreement between the
59	// orchestrator-resolved provider triple and the running subagent image â if
60	// they disagree, we refuse to dispatch (Pitfall 14 mitigation).
61	const vendorSentinel = "anthropic"
62	
63	// paramsAllowList enforces Q3 RESOLVED (03-RESEARCH Â§"Open Questions" line
64	// 933): unknown Provider.Params keys are rejected at startup. The fail-fast
65	// posture catches typos at apply time rather than letting them silently
66	// disappear into a passthrough. Add new keys here only after wiring the
67	// provider-side mapping that actually consumes them.
68	var paramsAllowList = map[string]bool{
69		"temperature":     true,
70		"thinking_budget": true,
71		"top_p":           true,
72		"top_k":           true,
73	}
74	
75	// nodeExtraCACertsPath is where the credproxy sidecar mounts its self-signed
76	// CA. NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS makes the claude CLI (a Node binary) trust the
77	// 127.0.0.1:8443 reverse proxy without disabling TLS verification globally
78	// (Phase 2 D-C2).
79	const nodeExtraCACertsPath = "/etc/tide/proxy/ca.crt"
80	
81	// Options configures the Anthropic subagent. Zero values pick sensible
82	// defaults for production use; tests override individual fields.
83	type Options struct {
84		// ClaudeBinary is the path to the claude CLI. Defaults to "claude" so
85		// the image's PATH resolves it.
86		ClaudeBinary string
87	
88		// WorkspaceRoot is the per-Project PVC mount point. The per-Task
89		// events.jsonl audit log lives at
90		// <WorkspaceRoot>/envelopes/<TaskUID>/events.jsonl per Phase 3 D-B4.
91		// Defaults to "/workspace".
92		WorkspaceRoot string
93	
94		// PricingOverrides is the D-02 per-instance price override map. On New(),
95		// these entries are merged over a clone of the compiled priceTable —
96		// the package-level priceTable is never mutated (T-14-02). Zero fields
97		// in an override entry derive from the existing compiled entry (if present)
98		// or from the override's non-zero fields. Cache fields are auto-derived
99		// from input when left as 0 (read=input/10, write=input*125/100).
100		PricingOverrides map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride
101	}
102	
103	// Anthropic implements pkg/dispatch.Subagent against the Claude Code CLI.
104	// Construct via [New]; instances are safe for concurrent calls because
105	// Run() makes no mutable struct state changes.
106	type Anthropic struct {
107		opts Options
108	
109		// prices is the per-instance effective price table, built by New() as
110		// maps.Clone(priceTable) merged with opts.PricingOverrides. It is read-only
111		// after construction, so concurrent Run() calls are safe. The package-level
112		// priceTable is never mutated (T-14-02 / Pitfall 2).
113		prices map[string]modelPrice
114	
115		// execFunc is the indirection seam that lets tests replace
116		// exec.CommandContext with a fixture-serving fake. Production calls
117		// New() which defaults this to exec.CommandContext; tests set it
118		// directly on the returned *Anthropic.
119		execFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd
120	}
121	
122	// New returns an *Anthropic configured with opts. Defaults: ClaudeBinary
123	// resolves "claude" via PATH; WorkspaceRoot is "/workspace".
124	//
125	// D-02: opts.PricingOverrides are merged over a clone of the compiled priceTable
126	// to build a per-instance effective table. The package-level priceTable is never
127	// mutated (T-14-02 / Pitfall 2).
128	func New(opts Options) *Anthropic {
129		if opts.ClaudeBinary == "" {
130			opts.ClaudeBinary = "claude"
131		}
132		if opts.WorkspaceRoot == "" {
133			opts.WorkspaceRoot = "/workspace"
134		}
135	
136		// Build the per-instance effective price table (D-02 / T-14-02).
137		// Clone first so we never write to the package-level var.
138		effective := maps.Clone(priceTable)
139		for modelID, override := range opts.PricingOverrides {
140			// Start from the existing compiled entry if available; otherwise zero.
141			base := effective[modelID]
142			if override.InputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
143				base.inputCentsPerMTok = override.InputCentsPerMTok
144			}
145			if override.OutputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
146				base.outputCentsPerMTok = override.OutputCentsPerMTok
147			}
148			// Cache fields: use explicit override value when > 0; otherwise derive
149			// from the (possibly just-updated) input rate.
150			if override.CacheReadCentsPerMTok > 0 {
151				base.cacheReadCentsPerMTok = override.CacheReadCentsPerMTok
152			} else if override.InputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
153				// Derive: cacheRead = 0.10 × input (same as Anthropic prompt-caching schedule).
154				base.cacheReadCentsPerMTok = override.InputCentsPerMTok / 10
155			}
156			if override.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok > 0 {
157				base.cacheWriteCentsPerMTok = override.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok
158			} else if override.InputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
159				// Derive: cacheWrite = 1.25 × input.
160				base.cacheWriteCentsPerMTok = override.InputCentsPerMTok * 125 / 100
161			}
162			effective[modelID] = base
163		}
164	
165		return &Anthropic{
166			opts:     opts,
167			prices:   effective,
168			execFunc: exec.CommandContext,
169		}
170	}
171	
172	// NewWithExec is the exported test seam: same defaults as [New] but takes an
173	// execFunc override so external packages (e.g. cmd/claude-subagent_test) can
174	// inject a fake exec without touching the unexported execFunc field. If
175	// execFunc is nil, defaults to exec.CommandContext (equivalent to [New]).
176	//
177	// Production code MUST use [New]. NewWithExec exists solely so the
178	// cmd/claude-subagent shim's tests can replicate the fake-`bash -c cat`
179	// fixture pattern from internal/subagent/anthropic/subagent_test.go without
180	// the shim having to live inside the anthropic package itself.
181	//
182	// D-02: opts.PricingOverrides are applied through [New] — the effective table
183	// is built before execFunc is overridden, so the pricing behavior is identical
184	// to the production path.
185	func NewWithExec(opts Options, execFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd) *Anthropic {
186		a := New(opts)
187		if execFunc != nil {
188			a.execFunc = execFunc
189		}
190		return a
191	}
192	
193	// Run satisfies pkg/dispatch.Subagent.Run. The dispatch flow:
194	//
195	//  1. Fail-fast vendor check: refuse if in.Provider.Vendor != "anthropic".
196	//  2. Fail-fast params allow-list (Q3): refuse unknown Provider.Params keys.
197	//  3. Load + render the compiled-in prompt template via
198	//     common.LoadPromptTemplate(in.Role, in.Level) â never read the host
199	//     filesystem for prompt content (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
200	//  4. Build `claude -p <rendered> --model <Provider.Model> --output-format
201	//     stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages --bare` (the --bare
202	//     flag is REQUIRED per RESEARCH Â§"Critical new finding").
203	//  5. Wire credproxy env: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL = in.ProxyEndpoint;
204	//     ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = in.SignedToken (never the raw key);
205	//     NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS = /etc/tide/proxy/ca.crt.
206	//  6. Tee stdout through ParseStream into events.jsonl at
207	//     <WorkspaceRoot>/envelopes/<TaskUID>/events.jsonl.
208	//  7. Assemble EnvelopeOut with parsed Usage + Result.
209	//
210	// Dispatch-level errors (I/O setting up events.jsonl, exec.Cmd start
211	// failure) return non-nil error. Task-level errors (claude exits non-zero)
212	// return (EnvelopeOut{ExitCode: N, Reason: ...}, nil) per pkg/dispatch
213	// godoc: "task-level failures (subagent exited non-zero) are expressed via
214	// EnvelopeOut.ExitCode and EnvelopeOut.Reason."
215	func (a *Anthropic) Run(ctx context.Context, in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut, error) {
216		// 1. Vendor fail-fast (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern: provider firewall).
217		if in.Provider.Vendor != vendorSentinel {
218			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: refusing vendor=%q (expected %q)", in.Provider.Vendor, vendorSentinel)
219		}
220	
221		// 2. Params allow-list fail-fast (Q3 RESOLVED â 03-RESEARCH line 933).
222		for key := range in.Provider.Params {
223			if !paramsAllowList[key] {
224				return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: unknown param %q (allowed: temperature, thinking_budget, top_p, top_k)", key)
225			}
226		}
227	
228		// 2.5. In-pod prompt read (defect #10b fix). If PromptPath is set, read the
229		// executor prompt artifact from the local namespace PVC and populate in.Prompt
230		// so step 3's template render resolves {{.Prompt}} correctly. This is the
231		// same-namespace read: the executor pod mounts the project PVC at WorkspaceRoot
232		// via subPath {project.UID}/workspace, so PromptPath is workspace-relative
233		// directly under WorkspaceRoot (NOT <root>/<uid>/workspace — the subPath
234		// already places the pod inside the project subtree). T-09-05 traversal defense:
235		// absolute paths and "../" escapes are rejected before any OS read.
236		if in.PromptPath != "" {
237			prompt, err := readPromptArtifact(a.opts.WorkspaceRoot, in.PromptPath)
238			if err != nil {
239				return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: read prompt artifact: %w", err)
240			}
241			in.Prompt = prompt
242		}
243	
244		// 3. Render the prompt template. Template content uses the EnvelopeIn
245		// shape as its execution context, so {{.Level}}, {{.TaskUID}},
246		// {{.Provider.Model}}, etc. are addressable.
247		tmpl, err := common.LoadPromptTemplate(in.Role, in.Level)
248		if err != nil {
249			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: load prompt template (role=%q, level=%q): %w", in.Role, in.Level, err)
250		}
251		var promptBuf bytes.Buffer
252		if err := tmpl.Execute(&promptBuf, in); err != nil {
253			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: render prompt template: %w", err)
254		}
255		renderedPrompt := promptBuf.String()
256	
257		// 4a. Compute + create the per-Task events dir BEFORE the args build: the
258		// claude invocation scopes its write capability to this dir via --add-dir
259		// (defect #7), and the planner child-CRD handoff (defect #5) writes to
260		// <eventsDir>/children/ -- the same dir readChildCRDs scans below.
261		eventsDir := filepath.Join(a.opts.WorkspaceRoot, "envelopes", in.TaskUID)
262		if err := os.MkdirAll(eventsDir, 0o755); err != nil {
263			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: mkdir events dir %q: %w", eventsDir, err)
264		}
265	
266		// 4b. Build the claude CLI invocation.
267		//   - --bare is REQUIRED per RESEARCH "Critical new finding": it skips
268		//     auto-discovery of the host claude config dir, .mcp.json, hooks,
269		//     skills, plugins, and any project CLAUDE-doc auto-memory. Hermetic by
270		//     construction.
271		//   - --permission-mode acceptEdits (defect #7) is the only headless mode
272		//     that auto-approves the Write/Edit tools; without it the planner
273		//     child-CRD Write is denied ("sandbox restrictions") and no children/
274		//     dir is ever created. bypassPermissions is sandbox-only; text-scraping
275		//     of Result was rejected for #5.
276		//   - --add-dir eventsDir (defect #7) scopes the granted write capability to
277		//     the per-Task dir only (minimal privilege -- NOT the whole workspace);
278		//     readChildCRDs traversal/Kind/empty-name guards remain the second line
279		//     of defense.
280		//   - The rendered prompt is delivered via STDIN, not as the -p positional
281		//     arg (defect #8): a large planner prompt as one argv entry risks Linux
282		//     MAX_ARG_STRLEN (128 KiB). claude -p reads the prompt from stdin when
283		//     the positional is omitted (stdin cap is 10 MB). There is no
284		//     --prompt-file flag in the pinned CLI.
285		args := []string{
286			"-p",
287			"--model", in.Provider.Model,
288			"--output-format", "stream-json",
289			"--verbose",
290			"--include-partial-messages",
291			"--permission-mode", "acceptEdits",
292			"--add-dir", eventsDir,
293			"--bare",
294		}
295		cmd := a.execFunc(ctx, a.opts.ClaudeBinary, args...)
296		cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(renderedPrompt)
297	
298		// 5. Credproxy env wiring (Phase 2 D-C1..C4). ANTHROPIC_API_KEY carries
299		// the HMAC-signed token, NEVER the raw API key -- the real key lives
300		// only in the credproxy sidecar Secret mount.
301		cmd.Env = append(cmd.Environ(),
302			"ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="+in.ProxyEndpoint,
303			"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="+in.SignedToken,
304			"NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS="+nodeExtraCACertsPath,
305		)
306	
307		// 6. Create the events.jsonl audit log. Phase 4 OpenInference parsing
308		// reads this file untouched; we tee every line through ParseStream as
309		// it arrives.
310		eventsPath := filepath.Join(eventsDir, "events.jsonl")
311		eventsFile, err := os.Create(eventsPath)
312		if err != nil {
313			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: create events.jsonl %q: %w", eventsPath, err)
314		}
315		defer func() { _ = eventsFile.Close() }() // best-effort event sink; close error is non-actionable cleanup
316	
317		// 7. Pipe stdout â ParseStream(stdout, events.jsonl). Stderr surfaces
318		// as task-level Reason on non-zero exit.
319		stdout, err := cmd.StdoutPipe()
320		if err != nil {
321			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: stdout pipe: %w", err)
322		}
323		var stderrBuf bytes.Buffer
324		cmd.Stderr = &stderrBuf
325	
326		if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
327			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: start %s: %w", a.opts.ClaudeBinary, err)
328		}
329	
330		usage, resultText, parseErr := ParseStream(stdout, eventsFile)
331		waitErr := cmd.Wait()
332	
333		// Decide exit code + reason. Order:
334		//   - parse error on the stream â dispatch-level (return err).
335		//   - wait error: task-level (ExitCode from exec.ExitError, Reason from stderr).
336		if parseErr != nil {
337			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: parse stream: %w", parseErr)
338		}
339	
340		// Compute cost from the per-instance effective price table before assembling
341		// EnvelopeOut. EstimatedCostCents flows into budget.RollUpUsage → Project.Status.budget.
342		// This call stays in internal/subagent/anthropic/ (provider firewall, D-C1).
343		// D-02: a.estimatedCostCents reads a.prices (the per-instance merged clone),
344		// never the package-level priceTable — T-14-02 race safety.
345		usage.EstimatedCostCents = a.estimatedCostCents(in.Provider.Model, usage)
346	
347		out := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{
348			APIVersion:  pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
349			Kind:        pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeOut,
350			TaskUID:     in.TaskUID,
351			Result:      resultText,
352			Usage:       usage,
353			CompletedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
354		}
355	
356		if waitErr != nil {
357			// Surface task-level failure via ExitCode + Reason (pkg/dispatch
358			// godoc contract). Do NOT return as a dispatch-level error.
359			out.ExitCode = 1
360			if exitErr, ok := waitErr.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
361				out.ExitCode = exitErr.ExitCode()
362			}
363			out.Reason = fmt.Sprintf("claude exit %d: %s", out.ExitCode, truncate(stderrBuf.String(), 256))
364			return out, nil
365		}
366	
367		// Planner file-handoff (defect #5): a planner dispatch (Role=="planner")
368		// authors child-CRD JSON files into <eventsDir>/children/*.json via the
369		// claude CLI's Write tool (the template instructs the exact path). The
370		// subagent pod has zero K8s verbs (Phase 2 D-A4) — this file handoff is the
371		// only channel from the model's output into EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs, which
372		// the controller materializes server-side. No text-scraping of Result.
373		// Executors (Role=="executor") emit no children; skip the read entirely.
374		if in.Role == "planner" {
375			// relPrefix is the workspace-relative children dir
376			// ("envelopes/<TaskUID>/children") stamped onto each child's SourcePath so
377			// the controller can set Task.Spec.PromptPath without knowing this pod's
378			// absolute PVC mount (defect #10b — prompt-as-PVC-artifact).
379			relPrefix := filepath.Join("envelopes", in.TaskUID, "children")
380			children, readErr := readChildCRDs(filepath.Join(eventsDir, "children"), relPrefix)
381			if readErr != nil {
382				// A malformed/poisoned children dir is a task-level failure, not a
383				// dispatch-level crash: the agent loop completed, but its structural
384				// output is unusable. Surface via ExitCode+Reason so the controller
385				// marks the parent Failed rather than retrying a clean dispatch.
386				out.ExitCode = 1
387				out.Reason = fmt.Sprintf("read child CRDs: %s", truncate(readErr.Error(), 256))
388				return out, nil
389			}
390			out.ChildCRDs = children
391		}
392	
393		return out, nil
394	}
395	
396	// truncate clips s to at most n bytes for embedding into EnvelopeOut.Reason.
397	// Reason is human-readable; full stderr lives in the Pod's container log.
398	func truncate(s string, n int) string {
399		if len(s) <= n {
400			return s
401		}
402		return s[:n] + "...(truncated)"
403	}
404	
405	// childKindAllowlist is the runner-side T-308 mitigation mirror of the
406	// controller's childKindAllowlist (internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go).
407	// Only these Kinds may flow from a planner's child-handoff files into
408	// EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs. A file declaring any other Kind poisons the whole
409	// batch — the controller enforces the same allowlist again at materialize
410	// time, but rejecting here gives a clearer task-level Reason and keeps a
411	// non-TIDE Kind from ever reaching the cluster's typed CRD graph.
412	var childKindAllowlist = map[string]bool{
413		"Milestone": true,
414		"Phase":     true,
415		"Plan":      true,
416		"Task":      true,
417		"Wave":      true,
418	}
419	
420	// readChildCRDs reads the planner's child-CRD handoff files from childrenDir
421	// (<workspaceRoot>/envelopes/<TaskUID>/children) and returns them as typed
422	// []ChildCRDSpec, in deterministic filename order.
423	//
424	// Contract (defect #5, file-handoff option a):
425	//   - Each *.json file in childrenDir is one ChildCRDSpec {kind, name, spec}.
426	//   - A missing childrenDir is NOT an error — it yields zero children (the
427	//     controller then surfaces the empty-output condition). This keeps an
428	//     executor-shaped or no-op planner run from failing on a clean exit.
429	//   - Path safety: only regular files whose resolved path stays within
430	//     childrenDir are read. Symlinks and any entry escaping childrenDir are
431	//     rejected (traversal defense) — the model's Write tool is constrained to
432	//     the per-task dir, but the runner does not trust that.
433	//   - Kind allowlist: every spec's Kind must be in childKindAllowlist.
434	//   - Name required: a child with an empty Name is rejected (the controller
435	//     uses it as metadata.name).
436	func readChildCRDs(childrenDir, relPrefix string) ([]pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec, error) {
437		entries, err := os.ReadDir(childrenDir)
438		if err != nil {
439			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
440				return nil, nil // no handoff dir → zero children, not an error
441			}
442			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read children dir %q: %w", childrenDir, err)
443		}
444	
445		// Canonical base for the traversal check. EvalSymlinks resolves the real
446		// directory so a symlinked childrenDir is still anchored correctly.
447		baseReal, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(childrenDir)
448		if err != nil {
449			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve children dir %q: %w", childrenDir, err)
450		}
451	
452		names := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
453		for _, e := range entries {
454			if e.IsDir() {
455				continue
456			}
457			if filepath.Ext(e.Name()) != ".json" {
458				continue
459			}
460			names = append(names, e.Name())
461		}
462		sort.Strings(names) // deterministic order for stable child materialization
463	
464		children := make([]pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec, 0, len(names))
465		var parseErrs []error
466		for _, name := range names {
467			full := filepath.Join(childrenDir, name)
468	
469			// Traversal defense: reject any entry that is a symlink or whose
470			// resolved path escapes baseReal. Lstat (not Stat) so we inspect the
471			// link itself, not its target. These are security boundaries — hard-abort,
472			// NOT per-file-skip (T-10-03-A mitigation).
473			info, lerr := os.Lstat(full)
474			if lerr != nil {
475				return nil, fmt.Errorf("lstat child file %q: %w", name, lerr)
476			}
477			if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
478				return nil, fmt.Errorf("child file %q is a symlink (rejected: traversal defense)", name)
479			}
480			if !info.Mode().IsRegular() {
481				return nil, fmt.Errorf("child file %q is not a regular file (rejected)", name)
482			}
483			realPath, rerr := filepath.EvalSymlinks(full)
484			if rerr != nil {
485				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve child file %q: %w", name, rerr)
486			}
487			if realPath != baseReal && !strings.HasPrefix(realPath, baseReal+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
488				return nil, fmt.Errorf("child file %q resolves outside children dir (rejected: traversal defense)", name)
489			}
490	
491			data, rderr := os.ReadFile(full)
492			if rderr != nil {
493				return nil, fmt.Errorf("read child file %q: %w", name, rderr)
494			}
495	
496			// Escape raw control characters that the model left unescaped inside
497			// string literals (Phase 10 medium-DoD cascade: multi-line title/desc
498			// values produced literal '\n'/'\t' bytes → "invalid character '\\n' in
499			// string literal"). Control chars OUTSIDE strings are valid JSON
500			// whitespace and are left untouched, so pretty-printed JSON still parses.
501			data = sanitizeJSONStringControls(data)
502	
503			// Use json.Decoder instead of json.Unmarshal so trailing prose after the
504			// closing } is ignored (Decoder stops at end of first JSON value). This
505			// is the observed production failure class: model appended explanatory
506			// text after }. json.Unmarshal would return "invalid character 'W'...".
507			var spec pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec
508			dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(data))
509			if jerr := dec.Decode(&spec); jerr != nil {
510				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("parse child file %q: %w", name, jerr))
511				continue
512			}
513			// Double-object detection (T-10-03-B mitigation): attempt to decode a
514			// second value from the stream. If a second full JSON value parses
515			// successfully, the file contains two concatenated objects — reject it so
516			// the second ChildCRDSpec cannot be silently injected. Trailing prose
517			// (e.g. "With these tasks we will...") is NOT valid JSON, so the second
518			// Decode returns a syntax error and we ignore it — trailing prose is
519			// the observed production failure class and must be tolerated.
520			var extra any
521			if extraErr := dec.Decode(&extra); extraErr == nil {
522				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("child file %q contains extra content after JSON object", name))
523				continue
524			}
525			// Kind/name validation: correctness checks, not security boundaries —
526			// per-file-skip (append error + continue) so a bad kind/name in one file
527			// does not lose its valid siblings.
528			if !childKindAllowlist[spec.Kind] {
529				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("child file %q declares disallowed kind %q (allowed: Milestone, Phase, Plan, Task, Wave)", name, spec.Kind))
530				continue
531			}
532			if spec.Name == "" {
533				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("child file %q has empty name", name))
534				continue
535			}
536			// Stamp the workspace-relative origin path so the controller can wire
537			// Task.Spec.PromptPath → this artifact (defect #10b). Not model-authored.
538			spec.SourcePath = filepath.Join(relPrefix, name)
539			children = append(children, spec)
540		}
541		if len(parseErrs) > 0 {
542			// Return valid siblings alongside the joined parse errors. The caller
543			// (Run) treats a non-nil readErr as a task-level failure — children that
544			// parsed successfully are still surfaced so the error message can name
545			// exactly which file(s) failed.
546			return children, errors.Join(parseErrs...)
547		}
548		return children, nil
549	}
550	
551	// sanitizeJSONStringControls escapes raw ASCII control characters (U+0000–
552	// U+001F) that appear INSIDE JSON string literals, leaving everything else —
553	// including control chars used as structural whitespace between tokens —
554	// byte-for-byte unchanged. LLM-authored child specs routinely contain literal
555	// newlines/tabs inside multi-line title/description values; the JSON spec
556	// requires those to be escaped, so a verbatim decode fails with "invalid
557	// character '\n' in string literal". This makes such files decodable without
558	// altering valid JSON (the escape of an already-valid document is a no-op).
559	//
560	// The scan tracks string-literal state with a backslash-escape toggle so a
561	// quote that closes a string is distinguished from an escaped quote inside one.
562	func sanitizeJSONStringControls(data []byte) []byte {
563		// Fast path: a document with no control bytes at all (e.g. compact
564		// single-line JSON) is already spec-valid — return it untouched.
565		hasControl := false
566		for _, b := range data {
567			if b < 0x20 {
568				hasControl = true
569				break
570			}
571		}
572		if !hasControl {
573			return data
574		}
575	
576		out := make([]byte, 0, len(data)+16)
577		inString := false
578		escaped := false
579		for _, b := range data {
580			if !inString {
581				out = append(out, b)
582				if b == '"' {
583					inString = true
584				}
585				continue
586			}
587			// Inside a string literal.
588			if escaped {
589				out = append(out, b)
590				escaped = false
591				continue
592			}
593			switch {
594			case b == '\\':
595				out = append(out, b)
596				escaped = true
597			case b == '"':
598				out = append(out, b)
599				inString = false
600			case b < 0x20:
601				// Raw control char inside a string → escape it.
602				switch b {
603				case '\n':
604					out = append(out, '\\', 'n')
605				case '\r':
606					out = append(out, '\\', 'r')
607				case '\t':
608					out = append(out, '\\', 't')
609				case '\b':
610					out = append(out, '\\', 'b')
611				case '\f':
612					out = append(out, '\\', 'f')
613				default:
614					const hex = "0123456789abcdef"
615					out = append(out, '\\', 'u', '0', '0', hex[b>>4], hex[b&0xf])
616				}
617			default:
618				out = append(out, b)
619			}
620		}
621		return out
622	}
623	
624	// promptArtifact is the minimal shape readPromptArtifact decodes from the
625	// children JSON file. It mirrors the ChildCRDSpec {kind,name,spec} envelope
626	// but only decodes spec.prompt so unrelated spec fields are ignored.
627	type promptArtifact struct {
628		Spec struct {
629			Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
630		} `json:"spec"`
631	}
632	
633	// readPromptArtifact reads the executor prompt from the workspace-relative
634	// promptPath under base (the in-pod WorkspaceRoot). It mirrors the traversal
635	// defense from FilesystemEnvelopeReader.ReadPrompt but uses base directly —
636	// the executor pod mounts the project PVC at WorkspaceRoot via subPath
637	// {project.UID}/workspace, so PromptPath is relative to WorkspaceRoot itself
638	// (NOT <WorkspaceRoot>/<uid>/workspace). T-09-05 mitigations:
639	//
640	//   - empty promptPath → error
641	//   - absolute promptPath → rejected
642	//   - filepath.Clean then ".." prefix check → traversal rejected
643	//   - resolved full path must stay under base (second-line defense)
644	//   - empty .spec.prompt → error (defect #4 class: no silent empty-prompt dispatch)
645	func readPromptArtifact(base, promptPath string) (string, error) {
646		if promptPath == "" {
647			return "", fmt.Errorf("empty promptPath")
648		}
649		if filepath.IsAbs(promptPath) {
650			return "", fmt.Errorf("promptPath %q must be workspace-relative, not absolute", promptPath)
651		}
652		clean := filepath.Clean(promptPath)
653		if clean == ".." || strings.HasPrefix(clean, ".."+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
654			return "", fmt.Errorf("promptPath %q escapes the workspace (rejected: traversal defense)", promptPath)
655		}
656		full := filepath.Join(base, clean)
657		// Second-line defense: resolved path must remain under base.
658		if full != base && !strings.HasPrefix(full, base+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
659			return "", fmt.Errorf("promptPath %q resolves outside the workspace (rejected)", promptPath)
660		}
661		data, err := os.ReadFile(full)
662		if err != nil {
663			return "", fmt.Errorf("read prompt artifact %q: %w", full, err)
664		}
665		var pa promptArtifact
666		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &pa); err != nil {
667			return "", fmt.Errorf("parse prompt artifact %q: %w", full, err)
668		}
669		if pa.Spec.Prompt == "" {
670			return "", fmt.Errorf("prompt artifact %q has empty .spec.prompt", full)
671		}
672		return pa.Spec.Prompt, nil
673	}

/workspace/worktrees/run-tide/run-dogfood-codex-runtime-1781739693/internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go

1	/*
2	Copyright 2026 TIDE Authors.
3

5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	// Package controller â dispatch_helpers.go consolidates the three planner
18	// dispatch helpers that all three up-stack reconcilers (Milestone, Phase,
19	// Plan) share (Phase 3 D-A1 / D-A2 / D-A4). The helpers exist to keep the
20	// reconciler bodies from drifting in lockstep â each reconciler is ~80-150
21	// LOC of NEW code instead of ~230 LOC because the shared bits live here.
22	//
23	//   - ResolveProvider walks the Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain
24	//     per D-C2: levels.{level}.{model,params} â Project default â
25	//     Helm-chart default.
26	//
27	//   - BuildPlannerEnvelope mirrors task_controller.go buildEnvelopeIn for
28	//     planner-level dispatch â sets Role="planner", Level=<level>,
29	//     populates Provider via ResolveProvider, and marshals to []byte.
30	//
31	//   - MaterializeChildCRDs server-side-creates child CRDs from
32	//     EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs. Enforces the Kind allowlist
33	//     (T-308 mitigation): only {Milestone, Phase, Plan, Task, Wave} pass.
34	//     Each child gets a controller-style owner ref pointing at the parent.
35	//     AlreadyExists is treated as idempotent success (mirrors Phase 2
36	//     SUB-03 / Pitfall F).
37	package controller
38	
39	import (
40		"context"
41		"encoding/json"
42		"fmt"
43		"maps"
44	
45		batchv1 "k8s.io/api/batch/v1"
46		apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
47		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
48		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
49		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types"
50		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
51		logf "sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/log"
52	
53		tideprojectv1alpha1 "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/api/v1alpha1"
54		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/reporter"
55		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
56	)
57	
58	// spawnReporterIfNeeded idempotently spawns the tide-reporter reader Job for a
59	// planner-level Job completion (Option C / T-09-13: AlreadyExists on Create is
60	// success; a pre-existing reporter Job means this completion was already
61	// observed). Returns isFirstCompletion=true when this is the initial
62	// observation of the planner Job's terminal state: either the reporter Job was
63	// newly spawned, or no ReporterImage is configured at all (stub/test path).
64	// When ReporterImage is set but the parent Project is unresolved, no spawn
65	// happens and isFirstCompletion stays false (mirrors the prior inline blocks
66	// in milestone_controller.go / phase_controller.go).
67	func spawnReporterIfNeeded(
68		ctx context.Context,
69		c client.Client,
70		scheme *runtime.Scheme,
71		parent metav1.Object,
72		project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project,
73		parentKind string,
74		reporterImage string,
75	) (bool, error) {
76		logger := logf.FromContext(ctx)
77		if reporterImage == "" {
78			logger.V(1).Info("skipping reporter Job spawn: ReporterImage not configured",
79				"parentKind", parentKind, "parent", parent.GetName())
80			return true, nil
81		}
82		if project == nil {
83			return false, nil
84		}
85		reporterJobName := fmt.Sprintf("tide-reporter-%s", parent.GetUID())
86		var existing batchv1.Job
87		if gErr := c.Get(ctx, types.NamespacedName{Name: reporterJobName, Namespace: parent.GetNamespace()}, &existing); gErr != nil {
88			if !apierrors.IsNotFound(gErr) {
89				return false, fmt.Errorf("get reporter job %s: %w", reporterJobName, gErr)
90			}
91			// Not found — spawn it (first completion observation).
92			reporterJob := BuildReporterJob(parent, project, defaultSharedPVCName, string(parent.GetUID()), parentKind,
93				ReporterOptions{ReporterImage: reporterImage}, scheme)
94			if cErr := c.Create(ctx, reporterJob); cErr != nil {
95				if !apierrors.IsAlreadyExists(cErr) {
96					return false, fmt.Errorf("create reporter job %s: %w", reporterJobName, cErr)
97				}
98			} else {
99				logger.Info("spawned reporter Job", "job", reporterJobName,
100					"parentKind", parentKind, "parent", parent.GetName())
101			}
102			return true, nil
103		}
104		logger.V(1).Info("reporter Job already exists; skipping spawn (T-09-13)", "job", reporterJobName)
105		return false, nil
106	}
107	
108	// ProviderDefaults carries the Helm-chart-supplied defaults used as the
109	// last fallback in ResolveProvider's precedence chain (D-C2).
110	//
111	// Image is the default subagent image ref (vendor selection). Models maps
112	// level name ("milestone"|"phase"|"plan"|"task") to the per-level default
113	// model identifier. Both are filled at Manager startup from Helm values.
114	type ProviderDefaults struct {
115		// Image is the default subagent image ref. Empty string means
116		// "no Helm default" â caller is responsible for surfacing this
117		// at Job creation time (a missing image is a config error).
118		Image string
119	
120		// Models maps levelâmodel. Missing key means "no Helm default for
121		// that level".
122		Models map[string]string
123	}
124	
125	// ResolveProvider walks Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain for the
126	// given level (D-C2). Returns a ProviderSpec with Vendor pinned to
127	// "anthropic" in v1.0 (per-vendor selection deferred â CONTEXT.md
128	// "Deferred Ideas"). Model and Params resolve via:
129	//
130	//	project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.<level>.Model â
131	//	project.Spec.Subagent.Model â
132	//	helmDefaults.Models[<level>] â
133	//	"" (caller surfaces missing-config error)
134	//
135	// Params merge: level Params copied first, then Project-level Params
136	// inserted only for keys NOT already set at the level â i.e., level
137	// wins on key conflict.
138	func ResolveProvider(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, level string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec {
139		// Helper to read per-level overrides.
140		var levelCfg *tideprojectv1alpha1.LevelConfig
141		if project != nil {
142			switch level {
143			case "milestone":
144				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Milestone
145			case "phase":
146				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Phase
147			case "plan":
148				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Plan
149			case "task":
150				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Task
151			}
152		}
153	
154		// Resolve Model.
155		var model string
156		switch {
157		case levelCfg != nil && levelCfg.Model != "":
158			model = levelCfg.Model
159		case project != nil && project.Spec.Subagent.Model != "":
160			model = project.Spec.Subagent.Model
161		default:
162			if helmDefaults.Models != nil {
163				model = helmDefaults.Models[level]
164			}
165		}
166	
167		// Merge Params â level overrides Project defaults on key conflict.
168		var params map[string]string
169		if levelCfg != nil && len(levelCfg.Params) > 0 {
170			params = make(map[string]string, len(levelCfg.Params))
171			maps.Copy(params, levelCfg.Params)
172		}
173		// (Project-level Params are not currently exposed on SubagentConfig
174		// â LevelConfig.Params is the per-level extension; if a future
175		// schema bump adds a top-level Subagent.Params, merge here with
176		// "level wins on conflict" semantics.)
177	
178		return pkgdispatch.ProviderSpec{
179			Vendor: "anthropic",
180			Model:  model,
181			Params: params,
182		}
183	}
184	
185	// BuildPlannerEnvelope constructs and marshals an EnvelopeIn for a
186	// planner-level dispatch. Mirrors task_controller.go:buildEnvelopeIn
187	// (Phase 2) but at the planner level: sets Role="planner",
188	// Level=<level>, populates Provider via ResolveProvider, and reuses the
189	// Caps / ProxyEndpoint / SignedToken parameters supplied by the caller.
190	//
191	// parent is the up-stack CRD whose UID stamps EnvelopeIn.TaskUID (the
192	// field is named TaskUID for backward-compat with Phase 2's envelope
193	// schema, but it carries the parent's UID at the planner level â the
194	// semantic is "the dispatch this envelope drives" regardless of level).
195	//
196	// prompt is the level-appropriate prompt body the dispatching reconciler
197	// supplies; it is assigned verbatim to EnvelopeIn.Prompt so the real
198	// subagent's template render has the actual outcome to author against
199	// (defect #4: prior to this the field was never set and the real Claude
200	// planner saw an empty prompt). token and prompt are DISTINCT params â
201	// token is the credproxy HMAC, prompt is the authoring instruction. The
202	// project planner passes Project.Spec.OutcomePrompt; deeper planners pass
203	// the same outcome (the parent artifact context â MILESTONE.md, the phase
204	// brief, PLAN.md â lives on the PVC and the template instructs reading it,
205	// and ParentName flows through EnvelopeIn.Dispatch).
206	// outcomePromptOf returns project.Spec.OutcomePrompt, nil-safe: the deeper
207	// reconcilers resolve the owning Project by walking the parent chain
208	// (resolveProject / resolveProjectForPlan), which returns nil on a not-yet-
209	// visible chain. A nil project yields "" — the same empty-prompt shape the
210	// real subagent already guards (EMPTY_PROMPT warning) rather than a panic.
211	func outcomePromptOf(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project) string {
212		if project == nil {
213			return ""
214		}
215		return project.Spec.OutcomePrompt
216	}
217	
218	func BuildPlannerEnvelope(level string, parent metav1.Object, project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, attempt int, token, prompt string, caps pkgdispatch.Caps, proxyEndpoint string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn, []byte, error) {
219		envIn := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{
220			APIVersion:    pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
221			Kind:          pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeIn,
222			TaskUID:       string(parent.GetUID()),
223			Role:          "planner",
224			Level:         level,
225			Prompt:        prompt,
226			Caps:          caps,
227			ProxyEndpoint: proxyEndpoint,
228			SignedToken:   token,
229			Provider:      ResolveProvider(project, level, helmDefaults),
230		}
231	
232		// Stamp parentName into the dedicated Dispatch metadata field so the stub
233		// planner can populate the child *Ref field (e.g. milestoneRef, phaseRef)
234		// without querying the K8s API â parent.GetName() is the authoritative
235		// source (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a secret). This is kept
236		// out of Provider.Params (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic runner's
237		// strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata.
238		envIn.Dispatch = &pkgdispatch.DispatchMeta{ParentName: parent.GetName()}
239	
240		data, err := json.Marshal(envIn)
241		if err != nil {
242			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn{}, nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal planner envelope: %w", err)
243		}
244		return envIn, data, nil
245	}
246	
247	// MaterializeChildCRDs delegates to reporter.MaterializeChildCRDs.
248	// The materialization machinery lives in internal/reporter so cmd/tide-reporter
249	// can import it without a back-edge to internal/controller (plan 09-04).
250	func MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, scheme *runtime.Scheme, parent metav1.Object, children []pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec) error {
251		return reporter.MaterializeChildCRDs(ctx, c, scheme, parent, children)
252	}
253	
254	// resolveImage walks Project.Spec.Subagent precedence chain for the given
255	// level, returning the resolved subagent container image reference.
256	//
257	//	Levels.<level>.Image → Spec.Subagent.Image → helmDefaults.Image → ""
258	//
259	// An empty return means no image was configured; callers must surface this
260	// as a config error rather than dispatching a Job with an empty image field.
261	// Level "project" has no entry in the switch (the CRD has no Levels.Project);
262	// resolution falls straight to Spec.Subagent.Image, then helmDefaults.Image.
263	func resolveImage(project *tideprojectv1alpha1.Project, level string, helmDefaults ProviderDefaults) string {
264		var levelCfg *tideprojectv1alpha1.LevelConfig
265		if project != nil {
266			switch level {
267			case "milestone":
268				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Milestone
269			case "phase":
270				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Phase
271			case "plan":
272				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Plan
273			case "task":
274				levelCfg = project.Spec.Subagent.Levels.Task
275			}
276		}
277		switch {
278		case levelCfg != nil && levelCfg.Image != "":
279			return levelCfg.Image
280		case project != nil && project.Spec.Subagent.Image != "":
281			return project.Spec.Subagent.Image
282		default:
283			return helmDefaults.Image
284		}
285	}
286	
287	// checkParentApproval implements the D-02 descent hold — children materialize
288	// but dispatch waits for parental approval (tidal lock pending).
289	//
290	// Returns (true, nil) when the direct parent is parked at AwaitingApproval,
291	// signalling that the child reconciler must hold Job dispatch with a 5s requeue.
292	// Returns (false, nil) when parentName is empty (root level — no parent to check)
293	// or when the parent is not found (client.IgnoreNotFound — NotFound is transient
294	// informer lag; callers continue dispatch and the next reconcile re-checks).
295	// Non-NotFound Get errors propagate to the standard requeue-on-error path.
296	//
297	// The parentKind switch covers "Milestone", "Phase", and "Plan" — the three
298	// parent kinds that can park at AwaitingApproval. Unknown kinds return (false, nil)
299	// (permissive default: unknown parent kind should not block dispatch).
300	//
301	// Design note: held children stay at Status.Phase="" — this helper writes NO
302	// status so tide approve's findAwaiting* cannot target a held child instead of
303	// the parked parent (Pitfall 5 from 12-RESEARCH.md).
304	func checkParentApproval(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, ns, parentName, parentKind string) (bool, error) {
305		if parentName == "" {
306			return false, nil
307		}
308		switch parentKind {
309		case "Milestone":
310			var ms tideprojectv1alpha1.Milestone
311			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ms); err != nil {
312				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
313			}
314			return ms.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
315		case "Phase":
316			var ph tideprojectv1alpha1.Phase
317			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &ph); err != nil {
318				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
319			}
320			return ph.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
321		case "Plan":
322			var plan tideprojectv1alpha1.Plan
323			if err := c.Get(ctx, client.ObjectKey{Namespace: ns, Name: parentName}, &plan); err != nil {
324				return false, client.IgnoreNotFound(err)
325			}
326			return plan.Status.Phase == "AwaitingApproval", nil
327		}
328		return false, nil
329	}

66	// RouteSpec is a (method, path-prefix) tuple used to extend the credproxy
67	// upstream allowlist for a Project (Phase 04.1 P4.2). The credproxy sidecar
68	// always enforces the hardcoded baseline (POST /v1/messages and
69	// POST /v1/messages/count_tokens); RouteSpec entries are additive extensions,
70	// never replacements.
71	type RouteSpec struct {
72		// Method is the HTTP method. Currently only GET and POST are accepted.
73		// +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=GET;POST
74		Method string `json:"method"`
75	
76		// PathPrefix is the URL path prefix. Must start with "/v1/" — paths
77		// outside the Anthropic API surface are rejected at admission.
78		// +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^/v1/[a-zA-Z0-9_/-]+$`
79		PathPrefix string `json:"pathPrefix"`
80	}

86		Name string `json:"name"`
87	
88		// RequestsPerMinute optionally caps API requests per minute for this provider.
89		// +optional
90		RequestsPerMinute *int32 `json:"requestsPerMinute,omitempty"`
91	
92		// TokensPerMinute optionally caps token throughput per minute for this provider.
93		// +optional
94		TokensPerMinute *int32 `json:"tokensPerMinute,omitempty"`
95	
96		// AllowedRoutes optionally extends the hardcoded credproxy allowlist
97		// (POST /v1/messages + POST /v1/messages/count_tokens) with additional
98		// (method, path-prefix) tuples. Use this to grant the subagent access
99		// to newly-released LLM endpoints (Files API, Search Tools, prompt
100		// caching) without rebuilding the credproxy image.
101		// Hardcoded safe defaults ALWAYS apply — operator additions are
102		// additive, never restrictive. Admin/billing paths are rejected at
103		// admission (defense in depth).
104		// +optional
105		AllowedRoutes []RouteSpec `json:"allowedRoutes,omitempty"`
106	}

226	// GitStatus records the per-Project git push state (Phase 3 D-B6).
227	//
228	// One branch per Project lifetime: BranchName is fixed at Project creation
229	// as "tide/run-<project-name>-<unix-epoch>" (Unix epoch keeps refnames
230	// colon-free per RFC 3987 refname rules). LastPushedSHA + LeaseFailureCount
231	// drive the --force-with-lease push contract: each push uses lastPushedSHA
232	// as the lease, increments leaseFailureCount on rejection, and resets to 0
233	// on success.
234	type GitStatus struct {
235		// BranchName is the lifetime branch fixed at Project creation.
236		// Format: "tide/run-<project>-<unix-epoch>".
237		// +optional
238		BranchName string `json:"branchName,omitempty"`
239	
240		// LastPushedSHA is the head SHA recorded on the most recent successful push.
241		// Used as the lease for subsequent `git push --force-with-lease`.
242		// +optional
243		LastPushedSHA string `json:"lastPushedSHA,omitempty"`
244	
245		// LeaseFailureCount tallies consecutive push lease rejections; reset to 0
246		// on successful push. Reconciler halts (Phase=PushLeaseFailed) when this
247		// count exceeds the configured retry budget.
248		// +optional
249		LeaseFailureCount int32 `json:"leaseFailureCount,omitempty"`
250	}

266		// WindowStart marks the beginning of the current rolling budget window.

269	}

271	// BoundaryPushStatus records the bounded auto-retry state of the project-level
272	// boundary push (debug defect #13b). The boundary push lands the already-
273	// integrated run branch on the remote AFTER the Project reaches Complete; it is
274	// observability + bounded recovery, NEVER a gate on Complete.
275	//
276	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a per-project retry tally object, NOT an
277	// aggregate schedule. Attempts is re-derived from .status on every reconcile so
278	// the bounded retry survives a controller restart (no in-memory counter).
279	type BoundaryPushStatus struct {
280		// Attempts is the number of boundary-push Jobs dispatched so far for this

282		// reaches the in-controller cap (maxBoundaryPushAttempts) — bounded retry,
283		// no push-loop.
284		// +optional

287		// LastAttemptTime is the timestamp of the most recently dispatched boundary

291	
292		// LastError carries the most recent boundary-push failure reason (from the
293		// push-result envelope) for operator visibility. Cleared on success.

350		MaxAttemptsPerTask int32 `json:"maxAttemptsPerTask,omitempty"`
351	
352		// Subagent declares provider+model selection for planner/executor dispatch
353		// (Phase 3 D-C2). When empty, Helm-chart defaults apply.
354		// +optional
355		Subagent SubagentConfig `json:"subagent,omitempty"`
356	
357		// Git declares the per-Project target repo + creds for artifact push
358		// (Phase 3 D-B6). Required for any Project whose lifecycle reaches push;
359		// optional in v1.0 only for purely transient/test Projects.
360		// Pointer so omitempty fully elides the field when absent — value-type
361		// GitConfig{} would serialize as `git: {repoURL: ""}` and trip the
362		// RepoURL pattern validation on existing Phase 2 test fixtures.
363		// +optional
364		Git *GitConfig `json:"git,omitempty"`
365	}
366	
367	// Project Phase constants for Project.Status.Phase (Plan 10 — init Job + budget gate).
368	const (
369		// PhasePending is the initial phase before any reconcile has run.
370		PhasePending = "Pending"
371		// PhaseInitialized is set when the init Job completes successfully.
372		PhaseInitialized = "Initialized"
373		// PhaseInitFailed is set when the init Job exits non-zero.
374		PhaseInitFailed = "InitFailed"
375		// PhaseBudgetExceeded is set when the project's absolute cost cap is exceeded.
376		PhaseBudgetExceeded = "BudgetExceeded"
377		// PhaseRunning is set when dispatch is actively proceeding.
378		PhaseRunning = "Running"
379		// PhasePushLeaseFailed is set when a push Job rejects due to --force-with-lease
380		// mismatch (Phase 3 D-B6). Recovery: kubectl annotate project
381		// tideproject.k8s/bypass-push-lease=true (mirrors D-D4 bypass-budget pattern).
382		PhasePushLeaseFailed = "PushLeaseFailed"
383		// PhasePushLeakBlocked is set when a push Job exits with code 10 (gitleaks
384		// finding — envelope.reason=leak-detected). Distinct from PhasePushLeaseFailed
385		// (exit-11) so the project_controller switch can fire
386		// tide_secret_leak_blocked_total only on the leak-class outcome (Phase 4 D-W1).
387		// Recovery: operator inspects the diff and either drops the leaked secret
388		// artifact or rotates the leaked credential, then clears the phase.
389		PhasePushLeakBlocked = "PushLeakBlocked"
390		// PhaseComplete is the terminal success phase — set when all Milestones
391		// reach Succeeded and the final push Job lands (Phase 3 D-B2 #4).
392		PhaseComplete = "Complete"
393	)
394	
395	// ProjectStatus defines the observed state of Project.
396	// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: NO aggregate schedule fields here.
397	type ProjectStatus struct {
398		// Phase is a high-level state label ("Pending", "Running", "Complete", "Failed").
399		// +optional
400		Phase string `json:"phase,omitempty"`
401	
402		// Conditions follow the standard K8s convention.
403		// +listType=map
404		// +listMapKey=type
405		// +optional
406		Conditions []metav1.Condition `json:"conditions,omitempty"`
407	
408		// Budget records running spend tallies (Phase 2+).
409		// PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: BudgetStatus is a tally object, not an aggregate schedule.
410		// +optional
411		Budget BudgetStatus `json:"budget,omitempty"`
412	
413		// Git records per-Project push state — branch name, last pushed SHA, and
414		// lease failure counter (Phase 3 D-B6). PERSIST-02 / Pitfall 4: this is a
415		// per-project tally object, NOT an aggregate schedule.
416		// +optional
417		Git GitStatus `json:"git,omitempty"`
418	
419		// BoundaryPush records the bounded auto-retry state of the project-level
420		// boundary push (debug defect #13b). NEVER gates Complete — surfaced via the
421		// non-terminal BoundaryPushed condition for observability + bounded recovery.
422		// +optional
423		BoundaryPush BoundaryPushStatus `json:"boundaryPush,omitempty"`
424	}

434	type Project struct {
435		metav1.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`

437		// metadata is a standard object metadata
438		// +optional
