9	
10	Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	import "time"
20	
21	// APIVersionV1Alpha1 is the envelope contract version shipped in TIDE v1alpha1.
22	// Consumers MUST reject envelopes whose apiVersion field does not match this
23	// constant via [ValidateAPIVersionKind].
24	const APIVersionV1Alpha1 = "tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1"
25	
26	// KindTaskEnvelopeIn is the "kind" discriminator for input envelopes written
27	// by the orchestrator and consumed by subagent images.
28	const KindTaskEnvelopeIn = "TaskEnvelopeIn"
29	
30	// KindTaskEnvelopeOut is the "kind" discriminator for output envelopes written
31	// by the in-pod harness and consumed by the controller on Job completion.
32	const KindTaskEnvelopeOut = "TaskEnvelopeOut"
33	
34	// EnvelopeIn is the self-contained input document written by the orchestrator
35	// to /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/in.json on the per-Project PVC. The
36	// subagent pod mounts the PVC read-only; everything the agent needs to run is
37	// in this document (D-A4). Out-of-tree subagent image authors decode this type
38	// via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] + json.Unmarshal.
39	type EnvelopeIn struct {
40		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1]. Harness MUST reject any
41		// other value via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] before consuming any field.
42		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
43	
44		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeIn]. Harness MUST reject any other value.
45		Kind string `json:"kind"`
46	
47		// TaskUID is the Kubernetes UID of the owning Task CRD object. Carried
48		// through to EnvelopeOut so the controller correlates result envelopes
49		// without scanning the PVC directory tree.
50		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
51	
52		// Role is the planner/executor selector: "planner" or "executor".
53		Role string `json:"role"`
54	
55		// Level is the hierarchy level this task operates at:
56		// "milestone" | "phase" | "plan" | "task".
57		Level string `json:"level"`
58	
59		// Prompt is the full prompt body the subagent should act on.
60		// For executor dispatches this field is empty — the executor reads the
61		// prompt in-pod from PromptPath off its own namespace PVC (defect #10b).
62		// For planner dispatches (role="planner") this field carries the resolved
63		// outcome prompt directly (the outcome prompt is not a PVC artifact).
64		Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
65	
66		// PromptPath is the workspace-relative path of the executor prompt artifact
67		// on the per-Project namespace PVC (e.g.
68		// "envelopes/<plannerUID>/children/task-01.json"). Non-empty only for
69		// executor dispatches (role="executor"). The in-pod anthropic runner reads
70		// the .spec.prompt field from this artifact and renders it as {{.Prompt}}
71		// in the compiled-in prompt template. The Manager MUST NOT read this path
72		// cross-namespace; the read happens in-pod so it uses the executor's own
73		// namespace PVC (defect #10b). Traversal-defended in-pod: absolute paths
74		// and "../" escapes are rejected before the read.
75		PromptPath string `json:"promptPath,omitempty"`
76	
77		// Branch is the per-run git branch (project.Status.Git.BranchName, format
78		// "tide/run-<project>-<unix>") the executor checks out its isolated worktree
79		// onto. Non-empty only for executor dispatches (role="executor"); planner
80		// dispatches short-circuit worktree creation (harness.EnsureWorktree). The
81		// in-pod executor reads this from in.json — the single source of truth for
82		// the worktree branch (09-09: replaced the never-written branch.txt).
83		Branch string `json:"branch,omitempty"`
84	
85		// FilesTouched is the set of repo-relative paths this task is expected to
86		// read or write. Used by the harness for output-path validation (HARN-05)
87		// and by the Plan admission webhook for file-touch consistency checks
88		// (PLAN-02).
89		FilesTouched []string `json:"filesTouched"`
90	
91		// DependsOn is the list of sibling Task names this task declares as
92		// predecessors in the execution DAG. Omitted when empty (D-A4).
93		DependsOn []string `json:"dependsOn,omitempty"`
94	
95		// DeclaredOutputPaths is the exhaustive list of PVC-relative paths the
96		// harness is permitted to write. Any write outside this set is rejected by
97		// the harness (HARN-05).
98		DeclaredOutputPaths []string `json:"declaredOutputPaths"`
99	
100		// Caps carries per-task resource limits enforced by the in-pod harness
101		// (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives these from Project.Spec.budget.
102		Caps Caps `json:"caps"`
103	
104		// ProxyEndpoint is the localhost HTTPS endpoint for the signed-token
105		// credential proxy sidecar (D-C1). Subagent sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to
106		// this value; the sidecar holds the real API key.
107		ProxyEndpoint string `json:"proxyEndpoint"`
108	
109		// SignedToken is the HMAC-SHA256 token minted by the controller at
110		// Job-create time. The credential proxy validates this token before
111		// forwarding API requests (D-C3).
112		SignedToken string `json:"signedToken"`
113	
114		// Provider selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
115		// subagent image should use to satisfy this dispatch (D-C3). Value type
116		// (not pointer): every dispatch declares a provider. The dispatching
117		// reconciler resolves Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,
118		// params} → Project-level defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the
119		// resolved triple here. The provider implementation in
120		// internal/subagent/{vendor}/ rejects an envelope whose Provider.Vendor
121		// does not match its compiled-in sentinel — fail-fast defense against
122		// image-tag-vs-envelope drift.
123		//
124		// Provider.Params carries MODEL parameters only (temperature, thinking_budget,
125		// …). It is NOT a general metadata bag — the anthropic runner hard-rejects
126		// any key outside its allow-list. Dispatch metadata the planner needs to
127		// build child-CRD refs lives in [EnvelopeIn.Dispatch], not here.
128		Provider ProviderSpec `json:"provider"`
129	
130		// Dispatch carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
131		// reads to build child-CRD references (e.g. parentName → projectRef /
132		// milestoneRef / phaseRef / planRef) without querying the K8s API. It is
133		// distinct from Provider.Params, which is model-parameters-only: overloading
134		// Provider.Params with dispatch metadata tripped the anthropic runner's
135		// strict param allow-list. Pointer + omitempty so executor-level and
136		// real-Claude dispatches that don't consume it don't serialize a
137		// "dispatch: null" placeholder. Real Claude-backed subagent images may
138		// ignore this struct entirely — the real planner authors from the outcome
139		// prompt, not canned parent refs.
140		Dispatch *DispatchMeta `json:"dispatch,omitempty"`
141	
142		// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata injected by integration tests.
143		// Real Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely
144		// (RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9 / D-F1). The field is omitted from JSON when nil
145		// so production envelopes are not polluted with "dev: null".
146		Dev *Dev `json:"dev,omitempty"`
147	}
148	
149	// EnvelopeOut is the result document written by the in-pod harness to
150	// /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/out.json. The controller reads this file
151	// after the K8s Job terminates to record Task completion status and budget
152	// tally (D-A2).
153	type EnvelopeOut struct {
154		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1].
155		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
156	
157		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeOut].
158		Kind string `json:"kind"`
159	
160		// TaskUID mirrors EnvelopeIn.TaskUID so the controller can correlate
161		// result envelopes without scanning directory names.
162		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
163	
164		// ExitCode is the subagent process exit code: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
165		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
166	
167		// Result is a human-readable one-line summary of the task outcome. Written
168		// by the harness on success or by the stub's canned response on testMode.
169		Result string `json:"result"`
170	
171		// Reason carries a structured failure code when ExitCode != 0, e.g.
172		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit", "output-path-violation", "token-expired".
173		Reason string `json:"reason"`
174	
175		// Usage is the token and cost tally for this task, rolled up by the
176		// controller into Project.Status.budget at Task completion (D-D2).
177		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
178	
179		// Artifacts is the list of PVC-relative paths the harness confirmed were
180		// written during this task's execution. Used for audit and for the
181		// declared-vs-actual output-path comparison (HARN-05).
182		Artifacts []string `json:"artifacts"`
183	
184		// CompletedAt is the wall-clock time the harness wrote this envelope.
185		CompletedAt time.Time `json:"completedAt"`
186	
187		// ChildCRDs is the slice of typed child-CRD declarations a planner
188		// subagent emits for the orchestrator to materialize server-side (D-A1).
189		// MilestoneReconciler reads Phase entries here; PhaseReconciler reads
190		// Plan entries; PlanReconciler reads Task + Wave entries; TaskReconciler
191		// (executor level) emits no ChildCRDs. Omitted from JSON when empty so
192		// executor-level EnvelopeOut documents stay small. Consumers MUST
193		// validate ChildCRDSpec.Kind against an allowlist before server-side
194		// create — threat T-308 mitigation lives at the consumer site (plan
195		// 03-08), not in this type.
196		ChildCRDs []ChildCRDSpec `json:"childCRDs,omitempty"`
197	
198		// Git carries git-side output a push Job or a harness produces, in
199		// particular the HeadSHA the per-Project run branch was advanced to at
200		// this dispatch's level boundary. Pointer + omitempty so dispatches that
201		// don't touch git (e.g. planner Jobs whose output is consumed only via
202		// child-CRD materialization) don't serialize a "git: null" placeholder.
203		Git *GitOutput `json:"git,omitempty"`
204	
205		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored. When
206		// reading the tiny termination-message status (PodStatusEnvelopeReader),
207		// this field is populated from the TerminationStub.ChildCount JSON key so
208		// planner controllers can perform race-free succession gating without also
209		// carrying the full ChildCRDs payloads (plan 09-08 Defect B). Zero for
210		// executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners. When reading a full
211		// out.json via FilesystemEnvelopeReader the field falls back to
212		// len(ChildCRDs) in the planner controllers (non-Option-C path).
213		ChildCount int `json:"childCount,omitempty"`
214	}
215	
216	// GitOutput carries git-side output fields a dispatch produces, populated by
217	// the push Job at level-boundary push success and by executor harnesses
218	// committing per-Task worktree changes (D-B4 / D-B6). HeadSHA is the SHA the
219	// per-run branch was advanced to; ProjectReconciler patches
220	// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA from this value to enable the next push's
221	// --force-with-lease check (Pitfall 13 mitigation).
222	type GitOutput struct {
223		// HeadSHA is the 40-character hex SHA the per-run branch points to after
224		// this dispatch's level-boundary push completed. Required when Git is
225		// non-nil.
226		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA"`
227	}
228	
229	// Caps carries per-task resource limits (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives
230	// these from Project.Spec.budget and injects them into every EnvelopeIn so
231	// the in-pod harness can enforce them without querying the K8s API.
232	type Caps struct {
233		// WallClockSeconds is the maximum wall-clock seconds the subagent is
234		// permitted to run before the harness sends SIGTERM.
235		WallClockSeconds int `json:"wallClockSeconds"`
236	
237		// Iterations is the maximum number of agent loop iterations before the
238		// harness sends SIGTERM.
239		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
240	
241		// InputTokens is the maximum cumulative input tokens across all LLM calls.
242		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
243	
244		// OutputTokens is the maximum cumulative output tokens across all LLM
245		// calls in this task.
246		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
247	}
248	
249	// Usage carries the token and cost tally reported by the harness in
250	// EnvelopeOut. Rolled up by the controller into Project.Status.budget at Task
251	// completion (D-D2).
252	type Usage struct {
253		// InputTokens is the total input tokens consumed across all LLM calls
254		// during this task's execution.
255		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
256	
257		// OutputTokens is the total output tokens produced across all LLM calls
258		// during this task's execution.
259		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
260	
261		// EstimatedCostCents is the estimated US-cent cost for this task, rounded
262		// up to the nearest cent. Used for Project.Status.budget.costSpentCents
263		// rollup.
264		EstimatedCostCents int64 `json:"estimatedCostCents"`
265	
266		// Iterations is the actual number of agent loop iterations completed.
267		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
268	
269		// CacheReadTokens is the count of input tokens served from the provider's
270		// prompt cache (D-C5). The Anthropic stream-json
271		// usage.cache_read_input_tokens field maps directly here. Surfacing this
272		// separately lets Project.Status.budget (Phase 2 D-D2) show the cached
273		// vs uncached input mix instead of hiding cache hits inside InputTokens.
274		CacheReadTokens int64 `json:"cacheReadTokens"`
275	
276		// CacheCreationTokens is the count of input tokens spent populating the
277		// provider's prompt cache during this dispatch (D-C5). The Anthropic
278		// stream-json usage.cache_creation_input_tokens field maps directly here.
279		// Cache creation is billed at a higher rate than cache reads; separating
280		// the two prevents budget rollups from understating real spend on
281		// cache-warmup turns.
282		CacheCreationTokens int64 `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`
283	}
284	
285	// DispatchMeta carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
286	// reads to build child-CRD references without querying the K8s API. It is kept
287	// separate from [ProviderSpec.Params] (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic
288	// runner's strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata. Real
289	// Claude-backed subagent images may ignore this struct — the real planner
290	// authors child CRDs from the outcome prompt, not canned parent refs.
291	type DispatchMeta struct {
292		// ParentName is the up-stack CRD's metadata.name. The stub planner stamps
293		// it into the child CRD's parent-spec ref (projectRef / milestoneRef /
294		// phaseRef / planRef) so the controller can wire ownership without the
295		// subagent holding a K8s client (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a
296		// secret).
297		ParentName string `json:"parentName,omitempty"`
298	}
299	
300	// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata for the stub-subagent image. Real
301	// Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely (D-F1 /
302	// RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9). It is intentionally kept in a sub-struct so the
303	// compiler makes omission explicit (pointer + omitempty on EnvelopeIn.Dev) and
304	// grep for "Dev" in production code is unambiguous.
305	type Dev struct {
306		// TestMode is the stub behavior selector. Valid values:
307		//   "success"              — writes canned result.json + declared artifacts; exit 0.
308		//   "fail-exit-1"          — writes structured failure result; exit 1.
309		//   "hang"                 — sleeps past caps.wallClockSeconds (HARN-02 test).
310		//   "exceed-output-paths"  — writes outside declaredOutputPaths (HARN-05 test).
311		// An empty string is treated as "success" by the stub.
312		TestMode string `json:"testMode,omitempty"`
313	}
314	
315	// TerminationStub is the tiny cross-namespace status carrier written to the
316	// dispatch Job's 4 KB termination message (Pod.status.containerStatuses[].
317	// state.terminated.message). It carries ONLY the fields the Manager needs to
318	// record Task completion without reading the PVC: ExitCode, Reason, Usage,
319	// the HeadSHA the run-branch was advanced to (if any), and ChildCount — the
320	// count of child CRDs the planner authored. ChildCRDs payloads and the verbose
321	// Result are intentionally excluded — they stay on the namespace-local PVC in
322	// out.json, which the in-namespace reporter Job reads (plan 09-05).
323	//
324	// ChildCount enables race-free succession gating (plan 09-08 Defect B): the
325	// controller compares observed owned children against this expected count and
326	// requeues until observed >= expected before declaring the level Succeeded.
327	//
328	// By construction this type MUST stay < 4 KB when marshalled; the test
329	// TestNewTerminationStub_StaysSmall enforces this invariant.
330	type TerminationStub struct {
331		// ExitCode mirrors EnvelopeOut.ExitCode: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
332		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
333	
334		// Reason mirrors EnvelopeOut.Reason: structured failure code (e.g.
335		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit") when ExitCode != 0.
336		Reason string `json:"reason"`
337	
338		// Usage mirrors EnvelopeOut.Usage: the token and cost tally for this
339		// dispatch, rolled up by the Manager into Project.Status.budget.
340		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
341	
342		// HeadSHA is the git HeadSHA from EnvelopeOut.Git.HeadSHA, flattened here
343		// to avoid a nested pointer. Empty when the dispatch did not produce a git
344		// push (executor-level, non-push Jobs). The Manager copies this into
345		// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA for the --force-with-lease fence.
346		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA,omitempty"`
347	
348		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored
349		// (len(EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs)). Used by the four planner-level controllers
350		// as the "expected" child count for race-free succession gating: a level
351		// cannot Succeed until observed owned children >= ChildCount (plan 09-08).
352		// Zero for executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners.
353		ChildCount int `json:"childCount"`
354	}
355	
356	// NewTerminationStub builds a TerminationStub from a full EnvelopeOut by
357	// copying the tiny-status subset: ExitCode, Reason, Usage, Git.HeadSHA, and
358	// ChildCount (= len(out.ChildCRDs)). ChildCRD payloads, Result, and Artifacts
359	// are deliberately excluded. A nil out.Git is safe and produces an empty
360	// HeadSHA. A nil or empty ChildCRDs slice produces ChildCount == 0.
361	func NewTerminationStub(out EnvelopeOut) TerminationStub {
362		headSHA := ""
363		if out.Git != nil {
364			headSHA = out.Git.HeadSHA
365		}
366		return TerminationStub{
367			ExitCode:   out.ExitCode,
368			Reason:     out.Reason,
369			Usage:      out.Usage,
370			HeadSHA:    headSHA,
371			ChildCount: len(out.ChildCRDs),
372		}
373	}
374	
375	// ValidateAPIVersionKind checks that apiVersion equals [APIVersionV1Alpha1]
376	// and that kind equals expectedKind. It is the first call an envelope consumer
377	// must make before accessing any other field (D-A3).
378	//
379	// Returns [*UnknownAPIVersionError] if apiVersion is unrecognized.
380	// Returns [*UnknownKindError] if kind is unrecognized.
381	// Returns nil on success.
382	func ValidateAPIVersionKind(apiVersion, kind, expectedKind string) error {
383		if apiVersion != APIVersionV1Alpha1 {
384			return &UnknownAPIVersionError{APIVersion: apiVersion}
385		}
386		if kind != expectedKind {
387			return &UnknownKindError{Kind: kind}
388		}
389		return nil
390	}

20	
21	// Subagent is the out-of-tree contract for TIDE subagent image runtimes
22	// (D-A1 / REQ-SUB-01). Out-of-tree image authors implement this interface to
23	// integrate a custom LLM backend into TIDE without modifying the orchestrator.
24	//
25	// In-repo reference implementations:
26	//   - cmd/stub-subagent — canned test responses, no LLM call (Plan 04).
27	//   - internal/harness  — cap enforcement + signed-token client + secret
28	//     redaction + output-path validation (Plan 06). The harness wraps a
29	//     concrete Subagent implementation selected at startup.
30	//
31	// The concrete Anthropic-backed implementation (Phase 3) satisfies this
32	// interface behind the provider firewall enforced by `make verify-dispatch-imports`.
33	type Subagent interface {
34		// Run executes the task described by in and returns the result envelope.
35		// The context deadline should be set to caps.wallClockSeconds + grace; the
36		// harness also enforces the cap internally via SIGTERM (HARN-02).
37		//
38		// A non-nil error indicates a dispatch-level failure (network, I/O);
39		// task-level failures (subagent exited non-zero) are expressed via
40		// EnvelopeOut.ExitCode and EnvelopeOut.Reason.
41		Run(ctx context.Context, in EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
42	}

17	package dispatch
18	
19	// ProviderSpec selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
20	// subagent image should use to satisfy a single dispatch (D-C3). It is carried
21	// on every EnvelopeIn so vendor + model are orthogonal axes from the
22	// orchestrator's point of view: the dispatching reconciler resolves
23	// Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,params} → Project-level
24	// defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the resolved triple here. The
25	// provider-side Subagent implementation (e.g. internal/subagent/anthropic)
26	// reads Provider.Model to drive its CLI invocation and rejects an envelope
27	// whose Provider.Vendor does not match the image's compiled-in sentinel —
28	// fail-fast defense against config drift between image tag and envelope
29	// content.
30	//
31	// Params is intentionally a string→string map (not a typed struct) so future
32	// per-vendor knobs (Anthropic thinking-budget, OpenAI temperature, Google
33	// safetySettings, etc.) can land without a pkg/dispatch schema bump every
34	// time. Validation of Params keys/values is the provider implementation's
35	// responsibility — pkg/dispatch only guarantees the bytes round-trip.
36	type ProviderSpec struct {
37		// Vendor is the provider sentinel string the subagent image checks at
38		// startup. Canonical values: "anthropic", "openai", "google", "xai",
39		// "opencode". Required (no omitempty) — every dispatch declares a vendor.
40		Vendor string `json:"vendor"`
41	
42		// Model is the per-vendor model identifier passed to the provider CLI or
43		// SDK (e.g. "claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5",
44		// "o1-mini"). Required (no omitempty).
45		Model string `json:"model"`
46	
47		// Params is the per-vendor tuning passthrough. Keys and values are
48		// vendor-specific; pkg/dispatch performs no semantic validation. Omitted
49		// from JSON when empty so dispatches that pass no tuning don't carry a
50		// "params: {}" placeholder.
51		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
52	}

63	// taskPlanRefIndexKey is the field indexer key for Task.Spec.PlanRef.
64	// Registered in SetupWithManager; used by listSiblingTasks.
65	const taskPlanRefIndexKey = ".spec.planRef"
66	
67	// outputPathsViolation is the EnvelopeOut.Result sentinel emitted when an
68	// executor wrote outside its DeclaredOutputPaths. Drives both the failure
69	// classification and the budget/metric reason mapping.
70	const outputPathsViolation = "output-paths-violation"
71	
72	// ErrParentUnresolved signals that resolveProject (or walkOwnerChainToProject)
73	// could not locate the owning Project via either the label fast-path or the
74	// bounded owner-ref chain walk. Callers convert this to a
75	// ConditionParentUnresolved status patch + 30s requeue. Phase 04.1 P1.4.
76	var ErrParentUnresolved = errors.New("no parent Project found via label or owner-ref chain")
77	
78	// TaskReconcilerDeps carries the dispatch-related dependencies for TaskReconciler.
79	// Mirrors HelmProviderDefaults precedent at dispatch_helpers.go:60-69.
80	//
81	// Fields are populated at Manager wiring time (cmd/manager/main.go) and never
82	// mutated thereafter — copying a small struct at construction is cheaper than
83	// indirection at every dispatch (RESEARCH.md §P3.2 §Known pitfalls).
84	//
85	// Pool fields (PlannerPool, ExecutorPool) and WatchNamespace stay as direct
86	// TaskReconciler fields because they're conceptually separate from "what to
87	// dispatch with" — they're concurrency limiters, not dispatch-tier deps.
88	type TaskReconcilerDeps struct {
89		Dispatcher     dispatch.Dispatcher
90		Budget         *budget.Store
91		Defaults       budget.Limits
92		SigningKey     []byte
93		CredproxyImage string
94		// SubagentImage is dead since Phase 13 — resolveImage owns resolution;
95		// retained for legacy test wiring, ignored at dispatch.
96		SubagentImage string
97		EnvReader     podjob.EnvelopeReader
98		Recorder      record.EventRecorder
99		// HelmProviderDefaults carry Helm-chart provider/model defaults, mirroring
100		// the Milestone/Phase/Plan reconcilers. buildEnvelopeIn uses them to resolve

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Working Rules
6	
7	Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.
8	
9	### 1. Observe First
10	
11	Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.
12	
13	1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
14	2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
15	3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
16	4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
17	5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
18	6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**
19	
20	Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.
21	
22	### 2. Execute, Don't Ask
23	
24	When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.
25	
26	**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**
27	
28	- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
29	- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
30	- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
31	- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
32	- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
33	- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
34	- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.
35	
36	### 3. Verify Before Claiming
37	
38	Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.
39	
40	Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:
41	
42	- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
43	- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
44	- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
45	- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
46	- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
47	- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
48	- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
49	- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)
50	
51	## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)
52	
53	Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.
54	
55	- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
56	- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
57	  ```
58	  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
59	  ```
60	  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)
61	- **`gsd-sdk query state.begin-phase` resets STATE.md body fields.** It re-derives `Current Position` and `Status` from current plan inventory. Edit body fields AFTER `state.begin-phase` runs, not before, or the edit gets clobbered.
62	- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
63	- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Filter dispatch with `--wave <new-gap-wave>` until any plan records APPROVED and the rewire fires.
64	- **Subagent prompts: focus, don't restate.** Agents have their own system prompts that cover anti-patterns and protocols. `<additional_context>` blocks should carry decision-only context (the specific gap, the user's choice, the corrected hypothesis) — not the full cascade history or the entire anti-pattern table. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
65	- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
66	- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
67	- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
68	- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
69	- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.
70	- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. Never let `make acceptance-v1-smoke` (it spins its OWN `tide-acceptance-<ts>` cluster) run while a `tide-test` cluster is still up — two single-node clusters OOM the node (exit 137). Phase 7's "env-gated, needs a bigger VM" deferral was actually solvable this way on the same 7.65 GiB host.
71	- **The acceptance `$0` path is a different deploy surface than Layer B — peel it one layer at a time.** Layer B fixtures carry a dummy `providerSecretRef` and use the test harness's helm install; the `$0` small-project has none and uses the chart defaults. So `$0` acceptance surfaces bugs Layer B masks (cascade-12 chart `default "latest"` image tags; cascade-13 credproxy hard-requiring `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Expect each green layer to expose the next; don't predict the terminator.
72	
73	## What this repository is
74	
75	**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.
76	
77	## The spec is load-bearing
78	
79	Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:
80	
81	- **Five-level hierarchy**: Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave. Each level has its own artifact (`MILESTONE.md`, phase brief, `PLAN.md`, diff, execution schedule) and dependency model. Don't collapse levels or invent new ones; if a real implementation pressure pushes back on the hierarchy, update the spec first.
82	- **Two distinct DAGs**: the *Planning DAG* (which artifacts must exist before another can be authored — shallow, fans out wide) and the *Execution DAG* (which code must exist before another can be written — deeper, fans out narrow). Same Kahn-layered algorithm runs on both. APIs and CRDs should keep these typed apart, not unified into one "DAG" abstraction.
83	- **Waves are derived, not declared**. They are the output of layered Kahn on the task DAG. The orchestrator never accepts a wave list as input — only a DAG. Re-deriving on every plan edit is intentional (the spec calls this out: O(V+E), cheap, no stale-schedule caching).
84	- **Cycles are bugs, not runtime conditions**. Cycle detection happens at plan-validation time and a cyclic DAG refuses to run. Don't add "cycle recovery" features; reject and surface.
85	- **Failure semantics at wave boundaries** (spec §"Failure handling at wave boundaries") are specific: failed task → siblings in same wave continue (they were declared independent), dependents in later waves never dispatch, non-dependents in later waves dispatch in strict-by-default but halt in conservative profile. Keep this contract intact when implementing the executor.
86	- **Resumption state is minimal**: indegree map + completed-task set. If the persistence layer starts wanting to store the full schedule, that's a smell — re-derive instead.
87	
88	## Vocabulary conventions
89	
90	The water/tide metaphor is intentional and consistent — use it in code names, CRD names, log lines, and docs:
91	
92	- Rising tide = planning wave fanning out across subagents
93	- Slack tide = review checkpoint between waves
94	- Tidal lock = phase whose dependencies have all resolved
95	- Tidepool = sub-DAG developed in parallel isolation
96	- TIDE pod = deployment unit running a TIDE orchestrator (the K8s pun is intentional and load-bearing)
97	
98	Prefer extending the metaphor naturally over coining unrelated terms. If a name doesn't fit, prefer plain prose.
99	
100	## Implementation guidance

## Working Rules

Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.

### 1. Observe First

Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. 5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`

- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log

One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)

## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)

- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
  ```
  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
  ```

- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.
- **Constrained-VM full-suite recipe (the dev Docker VM is ~7.65 GiB).** It DOES fit a clean `make test-int` if you don't accumulate state: delete → recreate → pre-warm (provisioner Ready + `kind load busybox:1.36`) a fresh kind cluster per heavy run, one heavy run at a time. Never let `make acceptance-v1-smoke` (it spins its OWN `tide-acceptance-<ts>` cluster) run while a `tide-test` cluster is still up — two single-node clusters OOM the node (exit 137). Phase 7's "env-gated, needs a bigger VM" deferral was actually solvable this way on the same 7.65 GiB host.
- **The acceptance `$0` path is a different deploy surface than Layer B — peel it one layer at a time.** Layer B fixtures carry a dummy `providerSecretRef` and use the test harness's helm install; the `$0` small-project has none and uses the chart defaults. So `$0` acceptance surfaces bugs Layer B masks (cascade-12 chart `default "latest"` image tags; cascade-13 credproxy hard-requiring `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). Expect each green layer to expose the next; don't predict the terminator.

## What this repository is

**TIDE** (Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution) — a Kubernetes-native orchestrator for hierarchical agentic coding work, designed to be open-sourced and to run against any project/codebase in any cluster. `README.md` is the design spec (and doubles as the public-facing README); check `git log` and `.planning/` for current implementation state.

## The spec is load-bearing

Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:

- **Five-level hierarchy**: Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave. Each level has its own artifact (`MILESTONE.md`, phase brief, `PLAN.md`, diff, execution schedule) and dependency model. Don't collapse levels or invent new ones; if a real implementation pressure pushes back on the hierarchy, update the spec first.

- **Cycles are bugs, not runtime conditions**. Cycle detection happens at plan-validation time and a cyclic DAG refuses to run. Don't add "cycle recovery" features; reject and surface.

Keep this contract intact when implementing the executor.
- **Resumption state is minimal**: indegree map + completed-task set. If the persistence layer starts wanting to store the full schedule, that's a smell — re-derive instead.

## Vocabulary conventions

The water/tide metaphor is intentional and consistent — use it in code names, CRD names, log lines, and docs:

- Rising tide = planning wave fanning out across subagents
- Slack tide = review checkpoint between waves
- Tidal lock = phase whose dependencies have all resolved

Prefer extending the metaphor naturally over coining unrelated terms. If a name doesn't fit, prefer plain prose.

100	## Implementation guidance
101	
102	- **Size planner and executor pools separately.** The spec argues planning fans out wide and execution fans out narrow — don't unify them into one worker pool.
103	- **Keep human-gate policy out of the controller.** Approve-every-milestone-but-auto-pass-plans must be as expressible as fully-autonomous or fully-supervised; the controller reads gate config, doesn't bake it in.
104	
105	(Provider/host/auth abstraction, pluggable subagent runtime, artifacts-as-source-of-truth, and resumability rules live in the Project Constraints and Stack Anti-patterns sections — don't restate here.)
106	
107	## Structural conventions in the spec document
108	
109	When editing `README.md` (the spec) itself:
110	
111	- Mermaid diagrams: nested `subgraph` containment for the planning graph; flat wave subgraphs with cross-wave edges for the execution graph. Match the existing style.
112	- Pseudocode uses Python-ish syntax with numbered-step comments (`# 1.`, `# 2.`).
113	- Worked examples follow pseudocode; the Kahn example walks the indegree map iteration-by-iteration. New algorithms get the same treatment.
114	- "Alternatives considered and rejected" is part of the doc's argumentative shape — when proposing a design choice, include the rejected alternatives, not just the winner.
115	- Voice is tight, declarative, em-dash-heavy. Match it rather than reverting to hedged corporate prose.
116	
117	<!-- GSD:project-start source:PROJECT.md -->
118	## Project
119	
120	**TIDE — Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution**
121	
122	A Kubernetes-native orchestrator that runs hierarchical agentic coding work as a topologically-sorted DAG of subagent dispatches. A human applies a `Project` CRD (outcome prompt + target repo + creds); TIDE authors `MILESTONE.md`, phase briefs, `PLAN.md` files, and task diffs by dispatching specialist subagents at each level, parallelizing across waves derived from the declared task DAG. Built to be open-sourced and portable across clusters from day one.
123	
124	**Core Value:** **The five-level paradigm (Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave) runs as a real K8s orchestrator that can drive its own next milestone end-to-end.** If everything else fails, TIDE-on-TIDE must work — that's what proves the paradigm and the implementation simultaneously, and it's the bar for "v1 ships."
125	
126	### Constraints
127	
128	- **Tech stack**: Go + sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime + kubebuilder — K8s ecosystem default, idiomatic for CRDs/controllers, best contributor pool.
129	- **Tech stack**: Pluggable subagent runtime via a documented container image contract — never hard-coded to Anthropic SDK; v1 ships with a Claude-backed concrete impl behind the interface.
130	- **Distribution**: Apache 2.0, Helm chart from v1, designed for installation in arbitrary clusters with no hidden host dependencies.
131	- **Portability**: No hard-coded git host (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea must all work behind a generic git remote), no hard-coded LLM provider, no hard-coded auth model — abstract behind interfaces.
132	- **Persistence**: CRD `.status` only for v1 — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-object size stays well under etcd's 1.5 MiB hard limit by keeping per-Task CRDs small and label-indexed.
133	- **Failure semantics**: Wave boundary contract from spec §"Failure handling at wave boundaries" must be preserved exactly — failed task → siblings continue, dependents in later waves never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile. Resumption state = indegree map + completed-task set, nothing more.
134	- **Resumability**: Long-running agentic work outlives single context windows. Every level boundary is a saved artifact; a fresh orchestrator restart re-derives waves from the task DAG + completed-task set in O(V+E).
135	- **Observability**: OpenTelemetry tracing must use OpenInference conventions for LLM/agent spans so traces are queryable in standard AI observability tools (Phoenix, LangSmith, Arize) without bespoke instrumentation.
136	<!-- GSD:project-end -->
137	
138	<!-- GSD:stack-start source:research/STACK.md -->
139	## Technology Stack
140	
141	Pinned versions. See `research/STACK.md` for rationale, alternatives, and citations.
142	
143	**Core:** Go 1.26 · controller-runtime v0.24.x · kubebuilder v4.14.0 · Kubernetes ≥ 1.33 (CEL CRD validation needs 1.29+) · Anthropic Go SDK v1.42.x · Claude Code CLI ≥ v2.1.139 · Helm 3 · Kustomize (kubebuilder-bundled).
144	
145	**Supporting:** Ginkgo v2.28 + Gomega · envtest · kind v0.31 · logr v1.4 + zap v1.28 · prometheus/client_golang v1.23 · otel v1.43 (trace stable; metrics still v0.65 — keep metrics on client_golang) · OpenInference attribute names emitted on OTel spans (no Go SDK) · go-chi v5 · go-git v5.
146	
147	**Dashboard:** React 18 + TypeScript · @xyflow/react v12 + dagre · Tailwind v4 · SSE (not WebSockets).
148	
149	**Dev tools:** controller-gen · setup-envtest · golangci-lint (gosec, errcheck, staticcheck on) · helmify (Kustomize → Helm at release).
150	
151	**Version coupling rules:**
152	
153	- Never bump `k8s.io/*` independently — let controller-runtime's `go.mod` dictate.
154	- Pin Anthropic SDK to a minor (`v1.42.x`); it rev-bumps weekly with beta surfaces.
155	- OTel trace API is v1.x stable; metric/log APIs are v0.x — don't conflate the trains.
156	- Pin kind node images by `@sha256` in E2E scripts.
157	
158	### Non-obvious choices to preserve
159	
160	- **Layered Kahn in stdlib**, not Gonum or dominikbraun/graph. The spec's exposition is iteration-by-iteration; a graph library obscures it.
161	- **Native K8s Jobs**, not Argo or Tekton. The orchestrator owns the DAG; waves are derived, not declared as Workflow templates.
162	- **CRD `.status` only** — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-Task CRD stays small; the indegree map is in-process and rederivable.
163	- **OpenInference attribute names on OTel spans**, not the OTel GenAI semconv (still pre-stable in 2026). Phoenix/LangSmith/Arize consume OpenInference today.
164	- **CEL CRD validation (`x-kubernetes-validations`)**, not admission webhooks — except for cycle detection if CEL can't express all-paths.
165	- **zap behind logr**, not slog (~3× hot-path win for field-heavy reconcile logs).
166	- **chi router** as `manager.Runnable`, not gin/echo/fiber (fasthttp won't compose with controller-runtime's manager).
167	- **React Flow + DOM nodes**, not Cytoscape canvas. DOM lets every node be a real React component with live status.
168	
169	### Anti-patterns
170	
171	- Don't mount host `~/.claude/` into executor containers. Use `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env from a K8s Secret.
172	- Don't use Claude Code's OAuth flow headless (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100).
173	- Don't accept a wave list as CRD input — only a DAG. Don't cache the schedule in `.status` — rederive from the completed-task set.
174	- Don't hard-code one LLM provider, one git host, or one auth model in the controller. All Anthropic-specific code lives behind the `Subagent` interface in `internal/subagent/anthropic/`.
175	- Don't replace layered Kahn with CPM/HEFT. If pools become heterogeneous, add a wave-internal sub-scheduler behind Kahn.
176	- Don't add cycle "recovery" features. Refuse a cyclic DAG with a clear error.
177	- Don't vendor GSD Markdown. Re-implement planner/executor prompts as compiled-in Go templates.
178	- Default the chart's `ServiceMonitor` to `prometheus.enabled=false` to avoid CRD-not-found on plain clusters.
179	<!-- GSD:stack-end -->
180	
181	<!-- GSD:conventions-start source:CONVENTIONS.md -->
182	## Conventions
183	
184	Conventions not yet established. Will populate as patterns emerge during development.
185	<!-- GSD:conventions-end -->
186	
187	<!-- GSD:architecture-start source:ARCHITECTURE.md -->
188	## Architecture
189	
190	Architecture not yet mapped. Follow existing patterns found in the codebase.
191	<!-- GSD:architecture-end -->
192	
193	<!-- GSD:workflow-start source:GSD defaults -->
194	## GSD Workflow Enforcement
195	
196	Before using Edit, Write, or other file-changing tools, start work through a GSD command so planning artifacts and execution context stay in sync.
197	
198	Use these entry points:
199	- `/gsd:quick` for small fixes, doc updates, and ad-hoc tasks
200	- `/gsd:debug` for investigation and bug fixing
201	- `/gsd:execute-phase` for planned phase work
202	
203	Do not make direct repo edits outside a GSD workflow unless the user explicitly asks to bypass it.
204	<!-- GSD:workflow-end -->
205

## Implementation guidance

- **Size planner and executor pools separately.** The spec argues planning fans out wide and execution fans out narrow — don't unify them into one worker pool.
- **Keep human-gate policy out of the controller.** Approve-every-milestone-but-auto-pass-plans must be as expressible as fully-autonomous or fully-supervised; the controller reads gate config, doesn't bake it in.

(Provider/host/auth abstraction, pluggable subagent runtime, artifacts-as-source-of-truth, and resumability rules live in the Project Constraints and Stack Anti-patterns sections — don't restate here.)

## Structural conventions in the spec document

When editing `README.md` (the spec) itself:

- Mermaid diagrams: nested `subgraph` containment for the planning graph; flat wave subgraphs with cross-wave edges for the execution graph. Match the existing style.
- Pseudocode uses Python-ish syntax with numbered-step comments (`# 1.`, `# 2.`).
- Worked examples follow pseudocode; the Kahn example walks the indegree map iteration-by-iteration. New algorithms get the same treatment.
- "Alternatives considered and rejected" is part of the doc's argumentative shape — when proposing a design choice, include the rejected alternatives, not just the winner.
- Voice is tight, declarative, em-dash-heavy. Match it rather than reverting to hedged corporate prose.

<!-- GSD:project-start source:PROJECT.md -->
## Project

**TIDE — Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution**

A Kubernetes-native orchestrator that runs hierarchical agentic coding work as a topologically-sorted DAG of subagent dispatches. A human applies a `Project` CRD (outcome prompt + target repo + creds); TIDE authors `MILESTONE.md`, phase briefs, `PLAN.md` files, and task diffs by dispatching specialist subagents at each level, parallelizing across waves derived from the declared task DAG. Built to be open-sourced and portable across clusters from day one.

**Core Value:** **The five-level paradigm (Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave) runs as a real K8s orchestrator that can drive its own next milestone end-to-end.** If everything else fails, TIDE-on-TIDE must work — that's what proves the paradigm and the implementation simultaneously, and it's the bar for "v1 ships."

### Constraints

- **Tech stack**: Go + sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime + kubebuilder — K8s ecosystem default, idiomatic for CRDs/controllers, best contributor pool.
- **Tech stack**: Pluggable subagent runtime via a documented container image contract — never hard-coded to Anthropic SDK; v1 ships with a Claude-backed concrete impl behind the interface.
- **Distribution**: Apache 2.0, Helm chart from v1, designed for installation in arbitrary clusters with no hidden host dependencies.
- **Portability**: No hard-coded git host (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea must all work behind a generic git remote), no hard-coded LLM provider, no hard-coded auth model — abstract behind interfaces.
- **Persistence**: CRD `.status` only for v1 — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-object size stays well under etcd's 1.5 MiB hard limit by keeping per-Task CRDs small and label-indexed.
- **Failure semantics**: Wave boundary contract from spec §"Failure handling at wave boundaries" must be preserved exactly — failed task → siblings continue, dependents in later waves never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile. Resumption state = indegree map + completed-task set, nothing more.
- **Resumability**: Long-running agentic work outlives single context windows. Every level boundary is a saved artifact; a fresh orchestrator restart re-derives waves from the task DAG + completed-task set in O(V+E).
- **Observability**: OpenTelemetry tracing must use OpenInference conventions for LLM/agent spans so traces are queryable in standard AI observability tools (Phoenix, LangSmith, Arize) without bespoke instrumentation.
<!-- GSD:project-end -->

<!-- GSD:stack-start source:research/STACK.md -->
## Technology Stack

Pinned versions. See `research/STACK.md` for rationale, alternatives, and citations.

**Core:** Go 1.26 · controller-runtime v0.24.x · kubebuilder v4.14.0 · Kubernetes ≥ 1.33 (CEL CRD validation needs 1.29+) · Anthropic Go SDK v1.42.x · Claude Code CLI ≥ v2.1.139 · Helm 3 · Kustomize (kubebuilder-bundled).

**Supporting:** Ginkgo v2.28 + Gomega · envtest · kind v0.31 · logr v1.4 + zap v1.28 · prometheus/client_golang v1.23 · otel v1.43 (trace stable; metrics still v0.65 — keep metrics on client_golang) · OpenInference attribute names emitted on OTel spans (no Go SDK) · go-chi v5 · go-git v5.

**Dashboard:** React 18 + TypeScript · @xyflow/react v12 + dagre · Tailwind v4 · SSE (not WebSockets).

**Dev tools:** controller-gen · setup-envtest · golangci-lint (gosec, errcheck, staticcheck on) · helmify (Kustomize → Helm at release).

**Version coupling rules:**

- Never bump `k8s.io/*` independently — let controller-runtime's `go.mod` dictate.
- Pin Anthropic SDK to a minor (`v1.42.x`); it rev-bumps weekly with beta surfaces.
- OTel trace API is v1.x stable; metric/log APIs are v0.x — don't conflate the trains.
- Pin kind node images by `@sha256` in E2E scripts.

### Non-obvious choices to preserve

- **Layered Kahn in stdlib**, not Gonum or dominikbraun/graph. The spec's exposition is iteration-by-iteration; a graph library obscures it.
- **Native K8s Jobs**, not Argo or Tekton. The orchestrator owns the DAG; waves are derived, not declared as Workflow templates.
- **CRD `.status` only** — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-Task CRD stays small; the indegree map is in-process and rederivable.
- **OpenInference attribute names on OTel spans**, not the OTel GenAI semconv (still pre-stable in 2026). Phoenix/LangSmith/Arize consume OpenInference today.
- **CEL CRD validation (`x-kubernetes-validations`)**, not admission webhooks — except for cycle detection if CEL can't express all-paths.
- **zap behind logr**, not slog (~3× hot-path win for field-heavy reconcile logs).
- **chi router** as `manager.Runnable`, not gin/echo/fiber (fasthttp won't compose with controller-runtime's manager).
- **React Flow + DOM nodes**, not Cytoscape canvas. DOM lets every node be a real React component with live status.

### Anti-patterns

- Don't mount host `~/.claude/` into executor containers. Use `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env from a K8s Secret.
- Don't use Claude Code's OAuth flow headless (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100).
- Don't accept a wave list as CRD input — only a DAG. Don't cache the schedule in `.status` — rederive from the completed-task set.
- Don't hard-code one LLM provider, one git host, or one auth model in the controller. All Anthropic-specific code lives behind the `Subagent` interface in `internal/subagent/anthropic/`.
- Don't replace layered Kahn with CPM/HEFT. If pools become heterogeneous, add a wave-internal sub-scheduler behind Kahn.
- Don't add cycle "recovery" features. Refuse a cyclic DAG with a clear error.
- Don't vendor GSD Markdown. Re-implement planner/executor prompts as compiled-in Go templates.
- Default the chart's `ServiceMonitor` to `prometheus.enabled=false` to avoid CRD-not-found on plain clusters.
<!-- GSD:stack-end -->

<!-- GSD:conventions-start source:CONVENTIONS.md -->

Conventions not yet established. Will populate as patterns emerge during development.
<!-- GSD:conventions-end -->

Before using Edit, Write, or other file-changing tools, start work through a GSD command so planning artifacts and execution context stay in sync.

Use these entry points:
- `/gsd:quick` for small fixes, doc updates, and ad-hoc tasks
- `/gsd:debug` for investigation and bug fixing
- `/gsd:execute-phase` for planned phase work

Do not make direct repo edits outside a GSD workflow unless the user explicitly asks to bypass it.

31		apierrors "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors"
32		metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
33		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
34		"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait"
35		"sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client"
36	
37		tidev1alpha1 "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/api/v1alpha1"
38		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/owner"
39		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
40	)
41	
42	// ErrParentUnresolved signals that PodJobBackend.Run could not locate the
43	// owning Project for the dispatched Task via the label fast-path or the
44	// bounded owner-ref chain walk. Callers (the calling controller) surface this
45	// as a ConditionParentUnresolved status patch + 30s requeue. Phase 04.1 P1.4.
46	var ErrParentUnresolved = errors.New("no parent Project found via label or owner-ref chain")
47	
48	// EnvelopeReader is the interface for reading an EnvelopeOut from the per-Project PVC.
49	// Injected into PodJobBackend for testability — production wiring uses
50	// FilesystemEnvelopeReader.
51	type EnvelopeReader interface {
52		// ReadOut reads /workspaces/{projectUID}/workspace/envelopes/{taskUID}/out.json.
53		// Returns a wrapped error if the file does not exist or cannot be parsed.
54		ReadOut(ctx context.Context, projectUID, taskUID string) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut, error)
55	}
56	
57	// PromptReader reads a per-Task executor prompt artifact from the per-Project PVC.
58	// Defect #10b: the prompt is a first-class PVC artifact (the children/<name>.json
59	// the Task was materialized from), not an inline CRD field. Kept separate from
60	// EnvelopeReader because reading the prompt requires the Manager's PVC mount,
61	// whereas EnvelopeOut is read from the completed pod's termination message —
62	// PodStatusEnvelopeReader cannot serve ReadPrompt.
63	type PromptReader interface {
64		// ReadPrompt resolves promptPath (workspace-relative, e.g.
65		// "envelopes/<plannerUID>/children/task-01.json") under the per-Project
66		// workspace and returns the executor instruction (the .spec.prompt field of
67		// that children JSON). Read FRESH on every dispatch so an edit to the file
68		// re-applies on re-dispatch with nothing to clobber.
69		ReadPrompt(ctx context.Context, projectUID, promptPath string) (string, error)
70	}
71	
72	// childPromptFile is the minimal shape ReadPrompt extracts the prompt from.
73	// It mirrors ChildCRDSpec's {kind,name,spec} but only decodes spec.prompt.
74	type childPromptFile struct {
75		Spec struct {
76			Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
77		} `json:"spec"`
78	}
79	
80	// FilesystemEnvelopeReader reads the EnvelopeOut JSON from the local filesystem.

1	# examples/projects/medium/project.yaml — Phase 5 D-B2 medium sample (~$5 real Claude).
2	#
3	# This sample lets operators run TIDE against real Claude (Haiku 4.5, cost-disciplined
4	# per D-B1) with a hard $5 cap — no external public repo dependency (D-B3). The
5	# targetRepo is an in-cluster http:// git remote served by the tide-git-http-server
6	# Deployment (git http-backend CGI + nginx), backed by the demo-remote-pvc populated
7	# by the demo-remote-init Job. All git transport is pure-Go (go-git HTTP) — no git
8	# binary in core images required.
9	#
10	# APPLY ORDER (do NOT apply this file alone — see README.md):
11	#   1. namespace.yaml
12	#   2. demo-remote-pvc.yaml
13	#   3. per-namespace-resources.yaml      (tide-projects PVC + tide-subagent SA)
14	#   4. Mirror tide-signing-key Secret from tide-system (see per-namespace-resources.yaml)
15	#   5. demo-remote-init-job.yaml         (init Job populates the bare repo on demo-remote-pvc)
16	#      kubectl wait --for=condition=Complete job/demo-remote-init \
17	#        -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=2m
18	#   6. git-http-server-deployment.yaml   (after init Job completes — RWO PVC ordering)
19	#      kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment/git-http-server \
20	#        -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=2m
21	#   7. kubectl create secret generic tide-secrets \
22	#        --from-literal=ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" \
23	#        --from-literal=GIT_PAT="" \   # empty — anonymous in-cluster http:// push
24	#        -n tide-sample-medium
25	#   8. project.yaml                      (THIS file)
26	#   9. kubectl wait --for=jsonpath='{.status.phase}'=Complete \
27	#        project/medium-project -n tide-sample-medium --timeout=30m
28	#
29	# Schema notes:
30	#   - ProjectSpec.OutcomePrompt is a first-class Spec field read by the planner subagent.
31	#   - targetRepo uses http:// in-cluster URL (file:// rejected by CEL validator in Phase 8).
32	#   - GIT_PAT can be empty — tide-push push mode GIT_PAT guard is scheme-conditional
33	#     (Phase 8 08-05 Task 3): empty PAT is accepted for anonymous http:// remotes.
34	---
35	apiVersion: tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1
36	kind: Project
37	metadata:
38	  name: medium-project
39	  namespace: tide-sample-medium
40	  labels:
41	    app.kubernetes.io/name: tide
42	    app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: tide-sample
43	    tideproject.k8s/sample: medium
44	spec:
45	  # In-cluster anonymous git remote served by git-http-server Deployment + ClusterIP Service.
46	  # Transport: go-git HTTP (pure-Go, no git binary in tide-push or claude-subagent images).
47	  # Service DNS: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
48	  # Short form (same namespace): http://git-http-server/demo-remote.git
49	  targetRepo: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
50	
51	  # Medium-sample outcome prompt — small enough to fit comfortably under the
52	  # $5 cap (D-B1) yet substantive enough to exercise TIDE's clone → plan →
53	  # execute → push loop end-to-end. ONE Phase, ONE Plan, target 3-5 tasks
54	  # (tight scope is the cost-control mechanism — same shape as the large
55	  # sample's Variant B per D-B4). Field is `spec.outcomePrompt` (added
56	  # 2026-05-23 root-cause fix; previously carried as annotation in Wave 3
57	  # Plan 05-12 — that workaround is now reverted).
58	  outcomePrompt: |
59	    The targetRepo is a tiny Go scaffold from examples/tide-demo-fixture/
60	    with a `Greeting(name) string` function plus a passing test.
61	
62	    Add a second exported function alongside `Greeting`: `FormattedNow() string`
63	    that returns the current time formatted as RFC3339. Update main.go to
64	    print both functions' output (one per line). Add a unit test for the
65	    new function that asserts the returned string parses back via
66	    time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ...).
67	
68	    Concrete deliverables (tight scope — ONE Phase, ONE Plan, target 3-5
69	    tasks):
70	
71	    1. main.go — add `FormattedNow()`; update main() to print both.
72	    2. main_test.go — add `TestFormattedNow` asserting round-trip parse.
73	
74	    Constraints:
75	    - DO NOT add external dependencies beyond the standard library.
76	    - DO NOT modify go.mod / go.sum.
77	    - Follow the existing repo conventions (MIT license header on new
78	      Go files; deterministic, side-effect-free function shape).
79	
80	    Pass criterion: `go test ./...` is green inside the cloned working tree.
81	
82	  # Phase 2 D-C1..C4: credproxy reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from this Secret.
83	  # The Secret is created BEFORE this CRD is applied (README.md step 7).
84	  # Same Secret carries GIT_PAT="" (empty — anonymous in-cluster http:// push
85	  # is accepted by the scheme-conditional guard in cmd/tide-push push mode).
86	  providerSecretRef: tide-secrets
87	
88	  # D-B1: $5 cap for the medium sample. If hit, dispatch halts with
89	  # Phase=BudgetExceeded (Phase 2 D-D2 + Phase 04.1 P4.1 rolling-window
90	  # infra). The tight outcome-prompt scope bounds expected spend well
91	  # under this cap — the cap is a safety net, not the operating budget.
92	  budget:
93	    absoluteCapCents: 500
94	    rollingWindowCapCents: 500
95	    # 24h rolling window per Phase 04.1 P4.1. metav1.Duration parses Go
96	    # duration strings; default would be 24h when unset; explicit per
97	    # Pitfall 7.
98	    rollingWindowDuration: 24h
99	
100	  # Phase 3 D-C2: cost-disciplined model pick per D-B1 spectrum. Haiku 4.5
101	  # is materially cheaper than Sonnet/Opus and adequate for the
102	  # function-addition outcome prompt above. Acceptance test uses Opus
103	  # (large sample); medium uses Haiku.
104	  subagent:
105	    image: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:1.0.0
106	    model: claude-haiku-4-5
107	
108	  # Phase 3 D-B6 per-project git target. TIDE pushes per-Phase / per-Plan /
109	  # per-Task / final-Project commits to a per-run branch on this remote.
110	  # Branch shape: tide/run-medium-project-<unix-ts>. NEVER pushes to main.
111	  #
112	  # The same `tide-secrets` Secret carries GIT_PAT="" (empty, accepted for
113	  # anonymous http:// remotes by the scheme-conditional guard in tide-push).
114	  git:
115	    repoURL: http://git-http-server.tide-sample-medium.svc.cluster.local/demo-remote.git
116	    credsSecretRef: tide-secrets
117	
118	  # All gates set to `auto` so the medium sample runs end-to-end without
119	  # human interruption. Operators inspecting the run watch
120	  # Project.Status.phase + the dashboard; gate-policy demos are a docs/
121	  # concern, not this sample's purpose.
122	  gates:
123	    milestone: auto
124	    phase: auto
125	    plan: auto
126	    task: auto
127	    pauseBetweenWaves: false
128	
129	  # Phase 2 D-E1: file-touch admission in strict mode. The PLAN.md the
130	  # planner authors must declare its file touches precisely; admission
131	  # rejects on mismatch.
132	  planAdmission:
133	    fileTouchMode: strict
134	
135	  # Phase 2 SUB-04 cap. 3 attempts per task is the v1 default.
136	  maxAttemptsPerTask: 3
